observations.recommendation: 5. Management directs the MPDO and Municipal Engineer as well as the concerned offices/end-users to prepare a comprehensive catch-up plan to expedite the implementation of these projects funded under the 20% Development Fund. Henceforth, ensure that proposed development projects undergo thorough feasibility studies, technical assessments, and procurement planning to ensure they are procurement-and-implementation-ready before funds are allocated in the ensuing budget year.

6. a. Management requires the concerned department heads to submit activity designs, including project’s success indicators to substantiate and justify the necessity of hiring the JOs and payment of wages in their office.
7. b. Management instructs the Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen the internal control system in hiring of additional workforce through job orders and limit the same for specific works which are of short duration.

16. Management ensures preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels for every vehicle and the Report of Fuel Consumption for evaluation.
17. Management to submit a copy of the specific internal control measures installed in connection with the disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants expenses.

18. Management implements immediately and accordingly the purpose of trust receipts, otherwise, evaluate the need of these unexpended balances of aid/financial assistance from different source agencies. Should these funds prove to be no longer needed, immediately return any unutilized balances to the source agency.

19. Management considers the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.

1. We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by strictly following the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had yet to implement the one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended that Management observe the requisition procedures for materials and the reporting on their issuance as contained in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure proper monitoring and accounting thereof, as well as to establish a reliable balance of the inventory account in the financial statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Engineering Office had yet to find the opportunity to implement this, as they were waiting for the new delivery of requested materials.

observations.recommendation: 11. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all collections are deposited intact with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Upon checking the records, some collections were not deposited on the following banking day.

observations.recommendation:
12. We recommended that Management close their TEEP checking account with LBP and return the remaining balance of the Trust Fund to the DepEd to avoid incurring future dormancy fees on the dormant bank account.
13. We also recommended that Management immediately close their two checking accounts for Philhealth and appropriate the money for the implementation of programs and projects that benefit the municipality's constituents, subject to the usual budgeting and accounting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management had agreed to the closure of the TEEP. However, no document had been submitted to the Auditor to confirm this.

observations.recommendation:
14. We reiterated our recommendation that the irregular procedure for granting cash advances, such as multiple cash advances to DOs in one day, be immediately stopped.
15. We also recommended that no additional cash advances be granted to the DOs until their previous advances are settled or properly accounted for.
16. We further recommended that Management consider appointing or designating additional DOs to avoid granting multiple cash advances to the incumbent DOs.
17. We furthermore recommended that the Municipal Treasurer set a cut-off date for the submission of approved payrolls by the different municipal offices every pay period, and require strict adherence thereto.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, records showed that there were still multiple cash advances granted on the same day. Despite the Order by the Municipal Mayor regarding the processing and cut-off date of the submission of payrolls, some workers had yet to comply, resulting in the late submission of payroll.

observations.recommendation:
18. We recommended that to avoid disapproval/invalidation of appointments, the HRMO-Designate ensure the following:
(a) Issue appointment under a permanent status only to a person who meets all the qualification requirements of the position to which he/she is being appointed, including the appropriate eligibility, in accordance with the provisions of law, rules, and standards promulgated in pursuance thereof; and
(b) The accuracy, authenticity, and completeness of all the requirements and supporting papers in connection with all cases of appointments are thoroughly reviewed and checked before they are submitted to the CSC.
observations.managementAction: The Appointment had yet to be resolved and was still on appeal at the Civil Service Commission RO-7, Cebu City.

observations.recommendation:
20. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update, balance, and certify the collections, deposits, and disbursements on the cashbooks, ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded therein, and henceforth adhere to the existing regulations on the handling, custody, and disposition of the cashbooks.
21. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the cash and cash equivalent account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had yet to complete the updating the cashbooks. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant had yet to completely reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation:
22. We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
23. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction: Management had yet to complete the preparation of the Road Network Plan. Management had yet to complete the conduct of the physical inventory of Road Networks.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that Management take immediate action to comply with the requirements stated in DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018, to obtain the required license for the ambulance. This will enable the Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the ambulance operates in accordance with the necessary standards and regulations established by DOH.
observations.managementAction: Management had encountered a problem with the availability of the TESDA schedule for competency assessment and training in First Aid for ambulance drivers and selected health workers.

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021. Otherwise, refund or replenish the LDRRMF for the cost of the subject motor vehicles and heavy equipment, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management wanted to be responsive to the needs of the people by providing safe modes of transportation. The LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment were still being used for purposes other than disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended that Management immediately submit to the Auditor the certification/documentation for the acquired two units of heavy equipment containing the required information stated under Section 4.5 of NBC No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the prescribed policy guidelines on the purchase of “secondhand/reconditioned” transport and heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO Designate could not be considered compliant with the required certification/documentation of the general obsolescence, the availability of the spare parts, the quality, degree, and extent of the repair done, and the maintenance requirements of the acquired heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation: 32. We recommended that Management submit the lacking supporting documents as required under Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to enable the Audit Team to properly evaluate the propriety and regularity of the fund transfers. We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment/transfer is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management had yet to submit the lacking supporting documents, such as the Audited financial reports for the past three years (CYs 2020, 2019, and 2018) of Bata ng Calabnugan, Inc. and the signed project proposal and sworn affidavit of the secretary of Casa Miani-Arvedi Foundation, Inc.

observations.recommendation: 33. We further recommended that the brand-new motor vehicle (Toyota Pick-up) be converted into a rescue vehicle equipped with the necessary equipment and apparatus and used exclusively for rescue and response activities. Management shall reimburse the LDRRMF for the cost of the vehicle from the General Fund if not complied.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had yet to convert its brand-new pick-up into a rescue vehicle equipped with the necessary equipment and apparatus, and used exclusively for rescue and response activities. Management had allocated funds to replenish the LDRRM Fund per SB Res. No. 208 under Appropriation Ordinance No. 6 series of 2022 and also the amount of ₱17,760.00 per SB Res. No. 266 under Appropriation Ordinance No. 8 series of 2022.

observations.recommendation:
34. We recommended that Management immediately submit to the DILG Regional Office No. VII the required written report on the acquisition of the brand new rescue vehicle which shall contain the details regarding the type, brand, engine displacement, quantity, price and seller of the purchased vehicle.
35. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations on the purchase/acquisition of government motor vehicles and attach the documentary requirements stated under Sections 5.4.1.1 to 5.4.1.5 of the DILG MC No. 2021-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had not attached to the written report a copy of the documentary requirements stated under Sections 5.4.1.1 to 5.4.1.5 of the DILG MC No. 2021-004.

observations.recommendation:
36. We recommended that Management submit immediately to the Audit Team the required permits to purchase and possess the subject radio communication equipment in compliance with NTC Memorandum Circular Nos. 2-05-88 and 11-21-88.
We further recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the abovementioned rules and regulations governing the acquisition of base and portable radio communication equipment.
observations.managementAction: The documentary requirements for the NTC application had yet to be complied with and had not been submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: 37. We recommended that the MDRRMC and MDRRMO review and update the MDRRMFIP annually to include projects and activities chargeable to prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to maximize the use of the fund to ensure that appropriate measures in disaster risk reduction and management are implemented.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, out of ₱51,085,135.04, only ₱19,653,991.38 or 38.47% had been utilized.

observations.recommendation: 40. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA to the nine PHWs: (a) Inter-Agency Task Force Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; (b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the SRA; (c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and (d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.
observations.managementAction: Verification of the documents that had been submitted showed that some of the 2019-nCOV Contact Tracing Sign and Symptom Log Form for the PUMs and PUIs lacked vital information such as Confirmed Case ID, Close Contact Name, Date of Last Exposure, and Date of Voluntary Quarantine Period. According to Management, they were not oriented; thus, they only filled in data they believed were needed for observation and monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly utilize the LDRRMF for programs/projects/ activities authorized under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and in accordance with DBM LBC No. 124 dated March 26, 2020. We also recommended that Management submit an explanation stating the reasons for the deficiencies observed.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO mentioned that the PPAs related to the 70% Mitigation Fund and Continuing Appropriations were properly implemented during the year. However, the disbursements were made in the succeeding year, which affected the fund utilization rate for CY 2024. He submitted a utilization report with remarks indicating which projects were paid for in 2025.

The Office of the Municipal Accountant had still lacked personnel who could focus on the reconciliation and they were not provided with the list of taxpayers and their amount due corresponding to the declared RPT and SET. There had yet to be a linkage of the iTax System to the Accounting Office, so maintaining subsidiary ledgers manually and reconciliation of the difference between the RPT and SET balances would entail a lot of work, which the Municipality could not perform yet due to a lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: 49. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by (a) conducting an intensive tax campaign in every barangay; (b) sending out notices of delinquencies; (c) posting a notice of the delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; and (d) publishing the notice of delinquency in a newspaper of general circulation. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer enforced collection measures, conducted tax campaigns, and posted notices; however, some beneficiaries could not be located.

observations.managementAction: Management still had to start taking the recommendation into account for the POWs of the succeeding projects of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: 52. observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copies of POs and contracts in CY 2024. However, the submission was still beyond the reglementary period of five working days from perfection.

observations.recommendation: 60. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the time deposit with the First Consolidated Bank, Dumaguete Branch to either Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160, DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, and COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The ₱1,000,000.00 time deposit with the FCB had been transferred to the Philippine Veterans Bank (PVB) on May 2, 2019, as authorized under Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 183 and Department of Finance Circular No. 01-2017, which included PVB as an AGDB, but the Auditor would like to emphasize that PVB’s authority to accept funds from NGAs/GOCCs/ LGUs was subject to limitations prescribed by the Monetary Board. The said time deposit should have been transferred to either LBP or DBP, where the authority to accept deposits from government agencies was absolute.

observations.recommendation: 61. We recommended that Management implement the important PPAs, such as the development and maintenance of database, construction of Women in Crisis Center and other programs to address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the planned programs/projects/activities for CY 2024 had not been completely implemented.

observations.recommendation: 62. We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of the delay. We further recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management had yet to completely implement the projects due to the late acquisition of road right-of-way, Deeds of Donation and appropriate location or area for the construction and some of the sites were unavailable or had a need for clearances and permits from concerned NGAs.

observations.recommendation: 63. We recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds to PPAs that are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned by RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management had not yet appointed personnel who would focus on complying with the recommendations. Other PPAs had yet to be started.

observations.recommendation: 64. We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had purchased a zero-waste Material Recovery Facility operated by Management and the Management had already closed the facility in CY 2023. The Municipality had not fully implemented the recommendation due to their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan being recently approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

observations.recommendation: 67. We recommended that the use of the three units cargo truck for ordinary, day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Trip tickets for the year still need to be submitted to see the usage of the LDRRM vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
68. We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality.
69. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.

The Local Chief Executive had created a team from the Municipal Legal Office to take charge of titling the Municipal real properties. The processing of transfer of title is ongoing. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer had already been maintaining property records for each real property.

observations.recommendation: 71. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the beneficiaries with outstanding loans pursuant to Section 3 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2001-3 to be immediately sent demand letters. 72. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct from the electric subsidy due the beneficiaries if they still are unable to pay.

The Office of the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitored and deducted from the subsidy due to beneficiaries to effect deduction from their outstanding EILP payable. Demand letters of some of the beneficiaries had already been sent but some beneficiaries could no longer be located; the Barangay Officials were assisting.

observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the Municipality did not procure any items from the PS-DBM. The BAC had received a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks, but it was not submitted to the Auditor for verification.

observations.recommendation: 74. We recommended that Management secure the necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is any, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: Management is still complying with the needed deeds and permits prior to implementing projects. The deeds and permits were being implemented and processed.

observations.recommendation: 76. Management terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; 77. Management should operate the Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same.

Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month.
79. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.

The Inventory Committee had not yet completed the physical count of properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

Eliminate the reciprocal accounts during the preparation of the financial statements recognizing assets and liabilities in accordance with Paragraph 7 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prepare and submit the CY 2024 RPCPPE immediately and henceforth ensure its timely preparation and submission in accordance with the provisions in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that management take the following actions:

Ensure that all reconciling items are immediately adjusted and recorded in the books to facilitate accurate monitoring and prevent difficulties in tracing discrepancies; and
Submit the JEVs and relevant supporting documents for any adjustments in the Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current and Savings accounts in CY 2024 and subsequent years.
Management to consider the reconvening or reestablishment of a Task Force Committee to investigate the long-outstanding Deposits in Transit, including:
Recovery of the related documents;
Issuance of communications (initial and follow-up) to accountable persons, informing them of their responsibilities and requesting payment where necessary; and
Initiation of administrative and criminal proceedings against responsible parties, if warranted.
Management to coordinate and follow up with the pertinent government servicing banks to obtain copies of bank snapshots, debit memos, and credit memos for reconciliation and verification.
The CAO and the CTO to work together to cancel existing stale checks and process the necessary adjustments to the Cash-in-Bank balance accordingly.

We recommended that management direct the CAO to continuously engage in efforts to reconcile the subject accounts. It is essential that they eliminate the noted variances and ensure that the financial statements accurately reflect the agency’s true financial position. Furthermore, we recommended that management implement stronger controls and oversight to prevent such discrepancies from recurring in the future.

Direct the CAO and the CBO to conduct a proper and accurate reconciliation of the actual amounts reported in the SCBAA and the SFPer to ensure consistency, transparency, and compliance with financial reporting standards; and
Expedite the migration of budget and financial records to the e-NGAS system to streamline reconciliation processes, enhance data accuracy, and improve overall financial management and reporting.

We recommended that management direct the City Accountant to investigate the cause of the variances and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile the financial reports. We also recommended that the LGU require the City Accountant conduct periodic reconciliation between the SSAF/SRE and the general/subsidiary ledger balances to ensure that the financial status and accountability reported in the financial reports are accurately and fairly presented.

We recommended that management take the following actions:
Ensure that CNA Incentive payments comply with the provisions of the Agreement unless such provisions are contrary to law, morals, good customs, public order, or public policy:

We recommended that management:

Discontinue payments to uniformed personnel from the Peace and Order Funds that lacks legal basis.

We recommended that management direct the City Engineer to:
Require the contractors to attach the written consent of bondsmen to any request for extension of contract time and ensure that the validity of the performance security is extended accordingly;
Ensure that payments of progress billings are adequately supported by the approved contract time extensions. These should be accompanied by proof of extension of the performance security, evaluation/investigative reports, and the corresponding PAGASA weather reports; and
Provide sufficient details in the "Justification" section of the evaluation/investigative reports, clearly outlining the basis for the contract time extensions.
We recommended that management ensure that payment of salaries to employees is supported by complete documentation, in strict adherence to the rules and regulations set forth under the law.

We recommended that management refrain from charging unrelated expenditures against the 5% LDRRMF and instead adhere to the provisions on the proper allocation and utilization of the fund, as outlined in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01, along with other relevant LDRRMF regulations, in order to prevent the risk of suspension or disallowance during audit and ensure the proper use of the fund for its intended purpose.

We recommended that management:
Continue to integrate the GAD Plan into its regular activities, while ensuring compliance with applicable GAD laws, policies, and regulations; and
Sustain efforts to address gender-related issues by implementing gender-responsive PAPs to achieve a higher utilization rate of the total GAD allocated budget.

Should these funds prove to be no longer needed, immediately return any unutilized balances to the source agency.

We recommended that Management consider the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.

We recommended that Management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 for the prevention of the incurrence of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, as soon as possible, its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation the AAR 2017. 

 It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2016.

1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update, balance, and certify the collections, deposits, and disbursements on the cashbooks, ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded therein, and henceforth adhere to the existing regulations on the handling, custody, and disposition of the cashbooks.
2. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the cash and cash equivalent account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer had already started updating the cashbooks.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant had already started reconciling their early records.

observations.recommendation:
3. We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
4. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction:
Management had already started preparing the Road Network Plan.
Management had already started preparing the Road Network Plan.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all collections are deposited intact with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 69(4) of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. (Reiterated under Finding No. 7 of this report.)
observations.managementAction: It has been a customary practice for the Municipal Accountant to request a reservation of official receipts so that the collections could be recorded within the year before the books are closed. Although the receipts were dated December 29, the actual collections were made in January, February, or March of the following year. These collections were subsequently deposited intact.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that Management take immediate action to comply with the requirements stated in DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018, to obtain the required license for the ambulance. This will enable the Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the ambulance operates in accordance with the necessary standards and regulations established by DOH.
observations.managementAction: Management encountered a problem with the availability of trainers for the TESDA Standard First Aid Competency Assessment and Training for ambulance drivers and selected health workers.

observations.recommendation:
11. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that sufficient and vital information, such as specific boundaries, stationing, landmarks, and symbols, are provided in the plans and drawings for road concreting and improvement projects and the construction of drainage canals.
12. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management unfailingly ensure strict compliance with the required detailed engineering activities under Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, giving utmost consideration to the deficiencies discussed above.
observations.managementAction:
Records showed that POWs of road concreting and improvement projects implemented in CY 2023 still lacked vital information.
In CY 2023, Management did not follow the relevant regulations.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommended that Management immediately convert the two pick-ups into rescue vehicles equipped with the necessary equipment and apparatus in accordance with DBM BC No. 2017-1 dated April 26, 2017.
15. We further recommended that Management require Toyota Mabolo Cebu, Inc. to mark the vehicles "For Official Use Only" (3 inches), under which should be written the corresponding name of the Office operating or using the same in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977, without additional cost to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The vehicles had still not been equipped with the necessary equipment and apparatus.
The vehicles were already marked “For Official Use Only” including the logo of the Municipality and the name of the office using the same.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021. Otherwise, refund or replenish the LDRRMF for the cost of the subject motor vehicles and heavy equipment, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
During CY 2023, the LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment were used for purposes other than disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021. Because Management wanted to be responsive to the needs of the people by providing safe modes of transportation.
During CY 2023, the motor vehicles and heavy equipment were used for purposes other than disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations.

observations.recommendation:
19. We recommended that Management strictly observe the documentary requirements on fund transfers in accordance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and require the MILHZ to furnish the LGU District Health Board a copy of the financial statement including a narrative report on the utilization of the revolving fund as required under Item III of the MOA.
We also recommended that Management strictly observe the following documentary requirements on fund transfers in accordance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001:
1. Copy of MOA/Trust Agreement from the Metropolitan Inter-Local Health Zone
2. Authorization from the Local Sanggunian for the Local Chief Executive to enter into a contract
3. Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses in the form of Work and Financial Plan from MILHZ for CY 2021
4. MILHZ Common Health Fund Utilization Report as of December 31, 2021 from the Office of the City Accountant, City of Dumaguete
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the following documents:
1. Copy of MOA/Trust Agreement from the Metropolitan Inter-Local Health Zone mentioned. This is still needed because even though the expenditure was already included in the Annual Budget under the Municipal Head Office under MOOE-ILHZ Municipal Counterpart, it was defined in generic name as "Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses".

observations.recommendation: 20. We recommended that Management immediately submit to the Auditor the certification/documentation for the acquired two units of heavy equipment containing the required information stated under Section 4.5 of NBC No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the prescribed policy guidelines on the purchase of “secondhand/ reconditioned” transport and heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) Designate of the Municipality submitted a Certification to the Auditor stating that as the end-user of the reconditioned equipment, they prefer the acquisition of such units due to security, low cost, reliability, and durability of technologies from Japan.
The MENRO Designate also certified that the parts of the type of garbage compactors acquired are readily available in Dumaguete City.
However, the mere Certification prepared by the MENRO Designate could not be considered compliant with the required certification/ documentation of the general obsolescence, the availability of the spare parts, the quality, degree, and extent of the repair done, and the maintenance requirements of the acquired heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents as required under Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to enable the Audit Team to properly evaluate the propriety and regularity of the fund transfers. We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment/transfer is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Management partially submitted the supporting documents for the NGO Fund Transfers.
However, there were still lacking some supporting documents such as the Audited financial reports for the past three years (CYs 2020, 2019, and 2018) of Bata ng Calabnugan, Inc. and the signed project proposal and sworn affidavit of the secretary of Casa Miani-Arvedi Foundation, Inc.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommended that the DILG Regional Office be furnished with the required written report on the acquisition of the brand new rescue vehicle which shall contain the details regarding the type, brand, engine displacement, quantity, price and seller of the purchased vehicle. Attached to the written report submitted were the Purchase Order, Acceptance and Inspection Report, Delivery Receipt, Certificate of Registration, and Official Receipt.
26. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations on the documentary requirements stated under Sections 5.4.1.1 to 5.4.1.5 of the DILG MC No. 2021-004.
We also recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations on the documentary requirements stated under Sections 5.4.1.1 to 5.4.1.5 of the DILG MC No. 2021-004.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Secretariat, noted by the BAC Chairman, submitted to the Regional Director of DILG Region VII through the DILG Municipal Local Government Operations Officer (MLGOO), detailing the type, brand, engine displacement, quantity, price, and seller of the purchased vehicle. Also attached to the written report submitted were the Purchase Order, Acceptance and Inspection Report, Delivery Receipt, Certificate of Registration, and Official Receipt. The MLGOO duly received the said report on June 17, 2022.
However, Management failed to attach to the written report a copy of the documentary requirements stated under Sections 5.4.1.1 to 5.4.1.5 of the DILG MC No. 2021-004.
No comment/action from Management.

observations.recommendation:
27. We recommended that Management submit immediately to the Audit Team the required NTC permits to purchase and possess the subject radio communication equipment in compliance with NTC Memorandum Circular Nos. 2-05-88 and 11-21-88.
28. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly to the abovementioned rules and regulations governing the acquisition of base and portable radio communication equipment.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRRM Officer submitted a letter dated June 21, 2022, to the OIC Regional Director of NTC Region VII indicating the Municipality’s intent to apply for Permits to Purchase and Possess another radio frequency exclusive to the LDRRM as the Municipality is using the existing frequency. The said letter was duly received by NTC-Negros Oriental on June 24, 2022.
Documentary requirements for the application still had to be complied with.

observations.recommendation: 29. We recommended that the MDRRMC and MDRRMO review and update the MDRRMFIP annually to include projects and activities chargeable to prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to maximize the use of the fund to ensure that appropriate measures in disaster risk reduction and management are implemented.
observations.managementAction:
In CY 2023, the total unexpended LDRRMF amounted to ₱32,331,878.52. Of this amount, only ₱27,064,717.62 was programmed for LDRRM activities, as shown in the LDRRMFIP for the year.
The MDRRMC and MDRRMO had not included in the CY 2021 MDRRMFIP the list of projects and activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years amounting to ₱13,948,840.59.

observations.recommendation:
31. We recommended that the following documents be immediately submitted to the Auditor to determine compliance with the conditions on the grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to wit:
a) Authority for the entitlement of the allowance;
b) Certificate of attendance during the ECQ period; and
c) IATF Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace.
Likewise, we recommended that Management submit (a) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance of the 485 Municipal personnel paid with COVID-19 Hazard Pay showing the official dates and hours they physically reported for work at their respective offices or work stations during the implementation of the ECQ period; and (b) Copy of the approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the following documents:
a) Authority for the entitlement of the allowance;
b) Copy of approved DTRs; and
c) Copy of the approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.
Risk Management also submitted Municipal IATF Resolution which was not fully responsive to the Auditor’s request.

observations.recommendation: 33. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA to the nine PHWs: a) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the SRA; c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the following documents:
a) Authority for the head of agency/office to render service during the period of the ECQ;
b) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance; and
c) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs.
However, verification of the documents submitted showed that some of 2019-nCOV Contact Tracing Sign and Symptom Log Form for the PUMs and PUIs lacked vital information such as, Confirmed Case ID, Close Contact Name, Date of Last Exposure, and Date of Voluntary Quarantine Period Ends. According to Management, they were not oriented thus, only filled up data they believed were needed for observation and monitoring.

observations.recommendation: 35. We recommended that: a) Management establish a procedure for identifying PHWs who shall be exposed to high risk or low risk hazard including the duration of such exposure duly approved by the Secretary of Health to support claims for hazard pay; and b) The Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks with certification from appropriate agencies and other documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
The MHO was able to get a Certification from the Secretary of Health with specified names of the PHWs.
The special order from the agency/department head did not specify the assignment to hazardous/difficult areas.
There was still no established procedure in place to determine who are exposed to risks, which risks are high or low, and how long the PHWs are exposed to such risks in support of the Hazard Pay claims.

The MTO continued to send Notices of Delinquency, conduct educational tax campaigns, and post Notices of Delinquency in conspicuous places.
The Office of the Provincial Treasurer, in collaboration with the respective Municipal Treasurer of the 19 municipalities in the Province of Negros Oriental, had already drafted an Action Plan in order to put on sale through public auction the delinquent properties in each municipality in accordance with the provisions of Section 256 of RA No. 7160. The said Action Plan was supposed to commence in the 1st quarter of CY 2020 but was deferred due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The bulk of the RPT Delinquency pertained to the Cooling Tower property owned by Energy Development Corporation (EDC) which had not been paid since CY 2015 because they invoked tax exemption. Court Decisions were made in favor of the Province of Negros Oriental but EDC appealed the case which is now pending a resolution.

observations.recommendation: 42. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by (a) conducting an intensive tax campaign in every barangay; (b) sending out notices of delinquencies; (c) posting a notice of the delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; and (d) publishing the notice of delinquency in a newspaper of general circulation. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The MTO continued to send Notices of Delinquency, conduct educational tax campaigns, and post Notices of Delinquency in conspicuous places.
The Office of the Provincial Treasurer, in collaboration with the respective Municipal Treasurer of the 19 municipalities in the Province of Negros Oriental, had already drafted an Action Plan in order to put on sale through public auction the delinquent properties in each municipality in accordance with the provisions of Section 256 of RA No. 7160. The said Action Plan was supposed to commence in the 1st quarter of CY 2020 but was deferred due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The bulk of the RPT Delinquency pertained to the Cooling Tower property owned by Energy Development Corporation (EDC) which had not been paid since CY 2015 because they invoked tax exemption. Court Decisions were made in favor of the Province of Negros Oriental but EDC appealed the case which is now pending a resolution.

observations.recommendation: 43. We recommended that Management cause the immediate completion of the project at no additional cost to the government so that it can be used for its intended purpose. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit proper accounting for the construction materials already procured but were not installed and desist from certifying as 100% completed any project unless he or his representative has conducted an inspection of the project.
observations.managementAction:
The canopy along the western and southern portion of the building and the roof framing and roofing materials in the western portion had been completely installed.
At present, the Industrial Park is being used as a quarantine facility and satellite office of the Municipality.
The Municipal Engineer was not able to submit to the Auditor the proper accounting for the construction materials already procured but not installed.

observations.recommendation: 44. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that detailed engineering is conducted before any POW is prepared and subsequently approved to refrain from making revisions which are not necessary in order that projects will be completed on schedule, so that the desired benefits can be immediately enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries as well as reduce the risk that programmed funds may no longer be sufficient due to price increases in construction materials and labor or changes in economic conditions.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the recommendation before all POWs of the projects implemented in CY 2023 were prepared.

observations.recommendation: 45. We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor copies of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to allow timely review.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted copies of POs and contracts together with copies of contracts in CY 2023. However, the submission was still beyond the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: 46. We recommended that the irregular procedure in the grant of cash advances, such as the grant of multiple cash advances to DOs in one day be immediately stopped. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer set a cut-off date for the submission of approved payrolls by the different Offices of the Municipality every pay period and require strict adherence thereto.
observations.managementAction:
Verification of records revealed that in CY 2023, DOs had been granted multiple cash advances in one day for the payment of salaries and wages, honoraria and other similar payments to employees and other personnel.
The Municipal Treasurer had already set cut-off dates for the payroll preparation.

observations.recommendation:
48. We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality thru the Administrative Council strictly adhere to the primary purpose of the technical and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality by limiting the grant of scholarship to out of school youths residing within the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 229, series of 2019.
49. We further recommended that the Council thoroughly review the individual credentials of the applicants to determine if they are eligible to be admitted to the scholarship program.
50. We finally recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation stating the reason(s) for a) admitting scholars who were neither out-of-school youths nor of school age; and b) accepting barangay certifications with altered dates of issue and submitting these as part of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction:
Records showed that the Municipality’s technical and vocational scholarship program had not been granted solely to out of school youths.
Ordinance No. 5 s. 2019 was passed in consonance with RA No. 10647. The HRMO Designate noted that the provisions thereof should have been anchored to Section 447(5)(xi) of RA No. 7160 which specifically states that the establishment of a scholarship fund shall be for the poor but deserving students residing within the municipality. The concerned personnel filed an appeal with the CSC Region VII on March 13, 2024 and they are still waiting for the decision. Moreover, she assured that they will comply with the provisions of the 2017 ORAOHRA, revised 2018 to avoid disapproval/invalidation of appointments in the future.
Management submitted a partial explanation and had already complied with certain requirements but not all issues were resolved.

observations.recommendation: 51. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer required the issuance of ORs from the leagues. The PCL had in May 2018 sent a letter to the National Printing Office (NPO) for the possible purchase of Accountable Form (AF) No. 51 and was informed that the concern was forwarded to the NPO Legal Team for the resolution of their request.
Payments for the year still need validation. The Municipality had yet to receive official receipts instead of printed receipts.

observations.recommendation: 52. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI on the CY 2018 AAR, and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor. Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the AAPSI for AAR 2022 was submitted by Management only on February 15, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
53. We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist establish the actual status of the breeding stock dispersal program by tracing the whereabouts and condition of the original breeding stocks and their offspring, if any, and render a report to the Municipal Accountant which includes the fair market value of each stock, for proper recording in the book of accounts.
54. In case of death/loss of assets, necessary adjusting entries shall be taken only after the issuance of Credit Notice arising from the result of the evaluation of the relief from accountability, if any.
55. We also recommended that proper disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with PPSAS 27 to provide information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
There is an on-going verification by the Municipal Agriculturist of the former MA’s records and tracing of the whereabouts and condition of original breeding stocks and their offspring.
Reduction or addition of the Work/Other Animals & Breeding Stocks account had not been regularly updated because of the failure of the Livestock Inspector to update the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 56. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the time deposit with the First Consolidated Bank, Dumaguete Branch to either Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160, DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, and COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The ₱1,000,000.00 time deposit with the FCB had been transferred to the Philippine Veterans Bank (PVB) on May 2, 2019, as authorized under Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 183 and Department of Finance Circular No. 01-2017 which included PVB as an AGDB. The Auditor would like to emphasize that PVB’s authority to accept funds from NGAs/GOCCs/ LGUs was subject to limitations prescribed by the Monetary Board. The said time deposit should have been transferred to either LBP or DBP where the authority to accept deposit from government agencies was absolute.

observations.recommendation: 57. We recommended that Management implement the important PPAs, such as the development and maintenance of GAD database, construction of Women in Crisis Center and the like, to address gender issues, other than the conduct of trainings/seminars/ orientations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality’s GAD database is already operational starting CY 2023.
However, the Municipality still had not designated an area for the Women in Crisis Center, which needs proper structure planning from the GAD focal point system.
Records showed that the planned programs/project/activities for CY 2023 had not been fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: 59. We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of the delay. We further recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.
observations.managementAction: The delay of implementation was due to the late acquisition of road right-of-way, Deeds of Donation and appropriate location or area for the construction. Also, some of the sites were unavailable or there was a need for clearances and permits from concerned NGAs.

observations.recommendation: 60. We recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds to PPAs that are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned by RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
Management had not appointed personnel who would focus on complying the recommendations.
Management had not yet assigned personnel who would focus on the implementation of LCPC programs.

observations.recommendation: 61. We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction:
The controlled dumpsite operated by Management was already closed in CY 2023. Management had not fully implemented the recommendation because their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission only on November 28, 2023.
Previously, the Municipality purchased a zero-waste Material Recovery Facility instead of opening and maintaining a sanitary landfill because they did not have enough funds.

observations.recommendation: 62. We recommended that Management properly allocate funds for the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We further recommended that Management immediately stop the use of GAD funds for scholarship program and, henceforth, prioritize the implementation of programs and activities which would promote gender equality.
observations.managementAction: Management had already assigned a GAD Coordinator to fast track the implementation of GAD projects, programs, and activities. However, records showed that in CY 2023, programmed projects had not been fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: 63. We recommended that the use of the three units cargo truck for ordinary, day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The military cargo trucks had been used as transportation service for the safety of the Municipal personnel assigned to deliver the necessary services, such as health, education, and construction of relevant facilities to the remote areas, steep mountains, and rough terrains.

observations.recommendation: 64. We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive had created a team from the Municipal Legal Office to take charge of titling the Municipal real properties. The processing of transfer of title is ongoing.
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer had already been maintaining property records for each real property owned by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: 66. We recommended that Management send demand letters to the beneficiaries with outstanding loans receivable of more than five years pursuant to Section 3 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2001-3. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct from the electric subsidy due the beneficiaries if they still are unable to pay.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters had already been sent to the EILP beneficiaries in CY 2017 thru the Barangay Officials. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitored and deducted from the electric subsidy due to beneficiaries to effect deduction from their outstanding EILP payable. However, some of the beneficiaries could no longer be located.

observations.recommendation: 67. We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Considering the expenses for transportation, labor, and per diems of personnel in picking up the items, the Municipality decided to purchase from suppliers outside PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: 68. We recommended that Management secure the necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is any, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of complying the recommendation. The concerned deeds and permits prior to implementing projects were still being processed.

observations.recommendation:
70. Management terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative.
71. Management should operate the Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same.
observations.managementAction: The operator had already been informed in writing regarding the MOA termination. Management is considering seeking legal advice regarding the contract termination.

observations.recommendation:
72. Prepare and submit immediately to the auditor the income statement and balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, by requiring the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary data/information and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month.
73. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: Management had already informed the operator about the recommendation and was waiting for their formal response prior to taking the next action. There was a partial submission of financial statements since the Manager only submitted the annual financial statements instead of the monthly report as required by the MOA. The Manager of the Bagsakan also failed to submit the monitoring and evaluation reports including the marketing strategies.

observations.recommendation:
74. The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all materials and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat. The stockroom should be secured against pilferage, unauthorized use and protected from deteriorating effects of the elements; The existing stockroom of the Municipality did not have enough space to accommodate all items procured by the Municipality.
75. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged in such a way as to facilitate retrieval when needed and ensure that fresh usable items are always available. Materials should be issued on first in, first out basis.
76. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored. Reconcile bin cards with the stock/property cards maintained by the stock clerk and with the physical count of stocks on hand;
77. The Office of the municipal treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article. All receipts, issuances and balances on hand of supplies and materials should be kept up-to-date. Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash advances granted to AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability covered by their fidelity bonds in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that in CY 2021, 21 bonded AOs, one AO renewed their fidelity bond late.
observations.reason: There was no reason given for the late renewal; some renewals were done 3 days after expiration date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer set a cut-off date for the submission of approved payrolls by the different Offices of the Municipality every other pay period and require strict adherence thereto.
observations.managementAction: Records revealed that in CY 2021, DOs were still granted multiple cash advances in one day.
observations.reason: No reason given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality require the issuance of ORs from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc.- Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to get printed receipts instead of official receipts for CY 2019 payments.
observations.reason: Records showed the leagues were still issuing printed receipts instead of official receipts (Accountable Form No. 51).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management secure necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is any, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality sent a demand letter to the property owners and was pursuing legal remedies.
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason for non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; and operate the Barangay Bagsakan Cooperative; train its staff to manage the same and require the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary monitoring and evaluation reports as well as report on marketing strategies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed the operator to maintain the required records and prepare marketing strategies; funds provided by DA were insufficient.
observations.reason: The operation of the BB project would entail possible expenses, especially in the construction of building, fixtures and storekeeping; the DA funds were not sufficient for profitable operation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
 a.	Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPEs in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability, as well as, to ensure timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor; and
b.	Require the Property Officer to maintain updated Property Cards to account for every PPE receipt and disposition, and to reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Refrain from granting or approving special monetization unless for valid and justifiable reasons, proof of which shall be attached and will form part of the documentary requirements for the application to avail of the said benefit; and,
Direct the HRMO to abide to the 50% limitation on the use of remaining Sick Leave credit balances in case of insufficient Vacation Leave balances available for special monetization.

We recommended and the Municipal DRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the required reports to COA and strictly adhere to the provisions on submission of reports under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended and Management agreed that complete details be provided in the Purchase Order, and the transactions be supported with complete documentation.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Local Finance Board  and  the  Municipal  Budget
Officer  evaluate  and  review  the  budget  proposals  well  before  it  is
submitted to the Local Sanggunian for enactment.  It is necessary  that  the
budget  be  thoroughly  reviewed  to  avoid  incidence  of  bloated   income
estimates which can affect sound financial management of the agency.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management reassess its credit  policies  to  ensure
the timely collection of receivables. Further, issue  billing  statement  to
the concerned persons and government agencies with outstanding  payables  to
Badiang Resort and Hotel.

Recommendation:

      We recommend  that  management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of  taxpayers  showing
the amount due and collectible  for  the  year  so  that  the  RPT  and  SET
Receivables can be adjusted by  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  reflect  the
correct balances at year-end.

Recommendation:


      We recommend that management submit to this Office the documents  that
prove  the  municipality’s  ownership  over  the  firetruck  otherwise,  its
relative repairs and maintenance, as well as its subsequent donation to  the
Bureau  of  Fire  Protection  shall  become  invalid.    Henceforth,   major
repairs/improvements that increase the useful life  and  efficiency  of  the
asset have to be capitalized.

We recommend that  management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
update the property  records  and  reconcile  with  that  of  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  facilitate  the  setting  up  and  periodic   provision   of
depreciation for infrastructure assets.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute the residual  value
at five percent of the acquisition  cost  in  determining  the  cost  to  be
depreciated from the time of the effectivity of COA Circular  No.  2017-004,
and adjust the net book value of depreciable assets to  fairly  present  the
affected accounts as of December 31, 2022. Copy  of  the  Adjusting  Journal
Entry/ies is recommended to be submitted to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that  management  require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare an adjusting entry to take up the interest earned during  the  year.
Henceforth,  the  Municipal  Treasurer  has  to  periodically  furnish   the
accountant a copy of the updated passbook/bank statement as basis to  record
the interest earned.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management require the BAC to immediately  stop  the
awarding of  projects/contracts  to  suppliers  with  conflict  of  interest
pursuant to Section 47.1 of RA 9184.

We recommended to the management to:
We recommended to the management to:
Immediately implement COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances to be able to update the property records of the city and eliminate the significant amounts of “beginning balances” or for reconciliation, “constant” entries, as well as negative balances in the property records.
Direct the City Accountant to maintain property, plant, and equipment ledger cards as required under Section 114 of NGAS for LGUs Vol. 1 and conduct a periodic reconciliation of the property and accounting records to bring both into agreement.

We reiterated to the management our previous years’ recommendations:
Submit immediately the subjected DVs; and
Exert more effort to submit on time the Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls and Official Receipts including its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to:
ensure that the recognition of accounts payable be supported with sufficient documentation indicating that the corresponding assets and expenses recorded were already received and incurred by the LGU;
prepare the appropriate adjusting entries with complete working papers and other appropriate supporting documents, and submit the same to the audit team, otherwise, forthcoming expenses to be charged to this “Account Payable” maybe disallowed in audit; and
for succeeding recognition of payables, ensure that all items are appropriately recorded into the proper accounts.

We recommended to the management to direct the Task Force Committee to conduct the necessary meetings to further the investigation of the Deposits in Transit, including a) recovery of the related documents; b) sending of communications to the persons accountable and informing them of their accountability as well as request for payment of the same; c) instituting of legal actions for nonfeasance.
Also, we recommended to the Task Force Committee to closely coordinate with the Audit Team as to the result of the investigations.

We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to ensure that subsidiary ledgers be prepared and maintained for the General Fund Proper, Economic Enterprise Fund, and Special Education Fund in adherence to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and NGAS for LGUs Vol. 2 as cited above.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations to the Management to require the City Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for the accounts maintained by LGU within the prescribed time mentioned in COA Circular No. 96-011 and to enroll with the LandBank weAccess which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% LDF, supported by a separate set of subsidiary ledgers for each development project charged to the said fund, whether current or continuing, and prepare financial statements exclusive for the 20% LDF, in order to effectively monitor existing fund balances, appropriations, and utilizations.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to provide the legal basis for and/or justify the disbursement of the COVID assistance.
Upon the submission of the legal basis, provide an explanation as to the differences in amounts given and require the Disbursing Officer to submit a justification or authorization letter for the payment of cash assistance that bears similar signatures.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management to instruct the personnel/department concerned of the following:
Preparation of an RIV or its equivalent in the procurement and requisition of fuel, the corresponding trip ticket/s should also be attached in the vouchers so that fuel consumptions are properly accounted for,
Use of the prescribed format of trip tickets,
Trip tickets should be completely and properly filled out especially the exact location/s for the travel, the approximate distance travelled, odometer readings at beginning and end of trip, so that the reasonableness of fuel procured/consumed can be determined,
Non-travel-related fuel disbursements should be supported with complete and sufficient documentation to establish its validity and propriety,
Preparation and submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Transportation to the Office of the Auditor,
Copies of trip tickets should also be attached in the fuel consumption reports, and
In case of variances between the quantity of fuel authorized for withdrawal and actual quantity of fuel withdrawn, appropriately remark the same in the RIV.
We also recommended to the management for the establishment of a policy indicating fuel estimates to be procured/ consumed for specific purposes and destinations, assignment of personnel to record and monitor the travels of each government vehicle as well as decide on the quantity of fuel to be withdrawn per trip with reference to his/her monitoring of fuel balances, and other relevant policies that could ensure the effective control and proper accounting of the LGU’s fuel consumption.

Recommendation:
