We recommended to the management to strictly enforce the submission of utilization/liquidation reports of fund transfers for projects where the City acts as the Implementing Agency in order to facilitate monitoring of such cash advances and to complete post audit of the same.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management to create specific guidelines in the giving of financial assistance to its constituents, taking the following into account, among others:
determination of amount of financial assistance
eligibility of the recipients
limit as to how many times an individual may apply for a financial assistance in a given period.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the City Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents on the monthly claim of salaries, RATA and PERA of the Sangguniang Panlungsod Members.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to direct:
the CTO to submit the RCI and the original cancelled checks, together with the DV and its supporting documents, to the CAO; and
the CAO to ensure that the original copy of the RCI and the cancelled checks are forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to:
submit the DTRs of the employees noted and communicated through AOM No. 2022-16-100(2021), and minutes of SP sessions to complete the post audit of said disbursements, and to
ensure that subsequent payments of RATA are supported with the documentary requirements noted above to support the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of
two units of brand new dump trucks and two units of reconditioned garbage
compactor trucks recorded to asset accounts Disaster Response and Rescue
Equipment and Motor Vehicles, respectively, to the proper accounts by making
the following adjusting entries:

Construction and Heavy Equipment ₱6,008,500.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱6,008,500.00
Construction and Heavy Equipment ₱4,499,000.00
Motor Vehicles ₱4,499,000.00

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management refer to the
description of accounts as a guide in recording expenses in the books of accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to avoid
erroneous entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the fund
transfer recorded to account Donations (5-02-99-080) to the proper account Due
from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations (1-03-06-030) by
making the following adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality:

Due from NGOs/POs ₱145,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱145,000.00

(Account Prior Period Adjustment was recommended because the books of
accounts as of December 31, 2021 were already closed.)

We also recommended that Management require the concerned NGOs
for the immediate liquidation of the funds received with the necessary
supporting documents and prepare the accounting entry to take up the
liquidation.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the documentary
requirements on fund transfers in accordance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 and require the MILHZ to furnish the LGU District Health Board
a copy of the financial statement including a narrative report on the utilization
of the revolving fund as required under Item III of the MOA.

We recommended that Management immediately submit to the Auditor
the certification/documentation for the acquired two units of heavy equipment
containing the required information stated under Section 4.5 of NBC No. 446-A
dated January 30, 1998.

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere strictly
to the prescribed policy guidelines on the purchase of
“secondhand/reconditioned” transport and heavy equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
supporting documents as required under Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012 to enable the Audit Team to properly evaluate the
propriety and regularity of the fund transfers.

We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure
the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the
Municipality before payment/transfer is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of
PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Cause the immediate reconstitution of the Inventory Committee;

b.	Direct the reconstituted Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPEs in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, as well as, to ensure timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor; and

c.	Require the Property Officer to maintain updated PCs to account for any PPE receipt and disposition, and to reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Accountant.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust efforts to retrieve the records of the PPEs with no details, and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to verify the details of the completed projects and immediately record the transfer of the project costs from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/ departments/ officials to:

a.	Hasten the implementation and completion of the remaining 2020 non-implemented development projects; and

b.	Thoroughly plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Development Plan/ Annual Investment Program and ensure that the same are feasible for implementation within the timeline.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

i.	Require the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	Take up in the appropriate inventory account the cost of supplies, materials and relief goods procured for distribution, and in the corresponding expense account the subsequent issuance/ distribution duly supported by the RIS and RSMI;

b.	Record in the books the inventory of welfare goods for distribution in custody of the Municipality based on the inventory report prepared; and

c.	Prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory of welfare/ relief goods procured by the Municipality.

ii.	Require the Supply/ Property Officer to:

a.	Coordinate with the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit an Inventory List/ Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution in custody of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020 in the prescribed format in Annex C; and

b.	Prepare and maintain Stock Cards, Requisition and Issue Slip, and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued for welfare goods.

iii.	Cause the submission of the duly validated/ approved list of beneficiaries, proof of receipt and distribution of welfare goods to individual beneficiaries/ recipients, as well as the Inventory List/ Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Cause the inclusion of a warranty clause in succeeding Contracts/ POs under Emergency Procurement of the Bayanihan Act, to ensure assurance of replacement of/ recovery on goods delivered with defects, and/ or do not conform to quality standards and technical specifications of the contract; and

b.	Ensure that all Contracts/ POs contain complete detailed item specifications pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to comply with the posting requirements in the GPPB Online Portal of all projects under the Bayanihan Act undertaken through Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases.

We recommend that management require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0058-B and 2020-0014A issued at the height of the pandemic.  Henceforth, require the end –users and the BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations on procurement at all times.
observations.managementAction: The concerned supplier of the medical supplies had already made a refund on the price difference cited in this particular audit observation.
observations.reason: A copy of the official receipt that was issued on the refund by the supplier was submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit explanation for resorting to reimbursement in the procurement of medical supplies and equipment costing P287,750.00, and henceforth, refrain from this procurement process.  Acquisition of goods and services is recommended to   pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant to R.A. 9184 and disbursement procedures in accordance with the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements were regulated and purchases of small value were subjected to an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.reason: Reimbursements were noted to be of non-material amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit to this Office those listed documents whose status of submission/attachment to the DV indicated a “NO”. Henceforth, for similar transactions, we recommend that management secure first the documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 prior to effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: The municipality pursued the titling of the purchased lot and is now waiting for the release of the title.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that for CY 2020 RATA totaling ₱2,812,500.00, management require the Municipal HRMO to submit Certification or the Daily Time Record (DTR) for the regular employees under the Executive Department; and the Municipal SB Secretary to submit certification of actual work/attendance of the official vis-à-vis the work schedule or performance of the local Sanggunian, to the Municipal Accountant.  Further, we also recommend that the same documents, already validated by the Municipal Accountant, be submitted to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: The payment of RATA is supported with DTRs for the regular employees and those under the Executive Department. As for the SB members, a certification of attendance of sessions was sought.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Budget was referred to/ subjected to the approval of the DILG before the same was adopted as final. This was done to ascertain that the activities/ projects addressed GAD-related issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant deduct applicable withholding taxes from payments to suppliers and remit the same to the BIR, in pursuance of the a forecited revenue regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to ensure that all payments for contracts of services were deducted with applicable taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2012-2014 LDRRMF back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the local Sanggunian for other social services.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of LDRRMF for the years 2016 and below were reverted to the General Fund.
observations.reason: The Schedule of Trust Liabilities-DRRM Program as of December 31, 2021 includes those balances from CY 2019-2021 only.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

 Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₱4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The payroll maker, through the Municipal Accountant, was directed to submit a list of employees of the LGU whose take home pay fall below ₱5,000.00 for the proper guidance of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer who signed the Loan Certifications.
observations.reason: Paid payrolls showed net pay of more than ₱5,000.00 per month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a written explanation/ justification on the foregoing observation. Henceforth, indicate the relevant description in the Purchase Request and Purchase Order of the goods to be procured.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation on the circumstance of the deficiencies in accomplishing the Purchase Orders.
observations.reason: There were instances of non-indication in the Purchase Orders of the relevant information/ data of the goods procured.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send billing statement to the concerned persons and government agencies with outstanding accounts at the Badiang Spring Resort and Hotel, while those who will be granted credit line be subjected to credit terms and conditions.
observations.managementAction: The BSRH Manager was instructed to gather the supporting documents for the reissuance of a billing statement to customers with outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: Collections were noted on some of the outstanding accounts. However, a low turn-over/ collection rate of receivables is observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.
observations.managementAction: Out of the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund (70% Disaster Preparedness Fund), the LGU was able to realize the conduct of standard first aid basic life support training-ambulance operations training, as well as the procurement of a surveillance drone.
observations.reason: The SAAOB showed material amounts of unimplemented projects (Capital Outlay) under the DRRM Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect from its households the applicable garbage fees, in accordance to Article F of the LGU’s Tax Ordinance No. 13-63.
observations.managementAction: Collection of garbage fees were made on a voluntary manner from the constituents who availed of the garbage collection services of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

1.  Management tap the assistance of barangay officials in ensuring that waste segregation is observed by its constituents. 

2.  Conduct activities to inform the constituents of their responsibility for waste reduction, recycling and composting, and for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer as the local waste management officer to monitor closely the implementation of the program among the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Waste segregation has to be ensured, or else, the receiving waste disposal facility in Albur town will not accept the waste to be disposed thereat. Although the amount involved was minimized, the LGU hired workers to do the task in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Payment of wages included those of job order workers tasked in waste segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert effort through the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the Accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Depreciable PPEs that were not subjected to depreciation in 2020 were already depreciated in 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management broaden its dissemination of the invitation to bid so that other willing suppliers would be made aware of the projects of the LGU.  Moreover, the management through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) have to welcome all capable bidders to be able to select the one which offers the most benefit to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Awarding of projects was undertaken even to a lone bidder provided that the bid submitted was responsive, as determined by the members of the Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 for the prevention of the incurrence of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The holding of various socio cultural activities in time with the fiesta celebration had been a practice of the LGU for a long time, not just to celebrate the occasion but to promote the town and its tourism potentials to visitors and balikbayans.
observations.reason: The activities relating to the celebration of the annual fiesta in 2021 were less than those of the preceding years that were pre-pandemic, yet, a substantial amount of disbursements for the same celebration was incurred.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend management to see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be promptly and fully implemented as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated thereby improving the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of various projects under the Continuing Appropriation of the 20% Development Fund (from prior years) were pursued only in 2021.
observations.reason: Completed projects under the 20% Development Fund were noted in 2021, however, most of these were funded out of the Continuing Appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, as soon as possible, its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation the AAR 2017. 

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: Printed AAPSI for the CY 2020 AAR was submitted to COA by management. An online posting in the website was not yet accomplished because the LGU’s website development and design is still on-going.

observations.reason: Subject for submission of the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to rectify, justify and/or submit the documents to substantiate all the deficiencies noted as well as submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation to conclude our audit on this matter.
observations.reason: Subject for submission of the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to instruct the City Accountant to: 
Discontinue recording collections received through banks as Cash-Local Treasury
Record subsequent disbursements of the SAP fund in the account “Donations.”
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to correct the erroneous entries and submit to the Office of the Auditor the working papers and other supporting documents.
observations.reason: Recommended JEV still not prepared by the CAO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
instruct the City Accounting Office to submit the lacking payrolls to the Office of the Auditor; and
contact the beneficiary who received the assistance twice to refund amount equivalent to amount received by one beneficiary.

We also recommended that for future liquidations of the same nature, ensure complete submission of supporting documentations in order not to delay the conduct of post audit.

We recommended to the Management to -  
 
Instruct the City Accountant to: 
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the adjusting entries, together with the working papers and supporting documents to correct the misclassifications and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers and submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation.; 
Record subsequent receipts of the ER 1-94 fund in the account “Share from National Wealth.”  
Instruct the City Budget Officer to include the balance of the ER 1-94 fund as source of fund for preparation of supplemental budget for the CY 2021 and make sure that the disbursements thereof are in accordance with Section 5.1 of DC No. 2020-040008 while the State of Public Health Emergency still exist. Receipts of ER 1-94 fund, once the health emergency is lifted, shall be considered in budget preparation and shall be guided by DC No. 2018-08-0021. 
Instruct the City Treasurer, City Accountant, and City Budget Officer to coordinate with each other every time new fund is received by the LGU so that its nature can be properly determined in order to undertake necessary actions before, during, and after its disbursement; 
To submit explanation/justification for all the noted deficiencies as discussed above.
observations.reason: Recommended JEV still not prepared by the CAO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to direct the Accountant to:
Submit the lacking documentation to support the utilization of the above-cited disbursements, 
Ensure that supplies and materials are recorded as inventory, and that the utilization are properly supported with complete documentation, and for construction materials to be supported with detailed plans and inspection reports after the completion of the project,
Prepare adjusting entries for the above-cited transactions that should be recorded as expense and inventory.
Record future disbursements of the same nature in an asset/inventory account.
observations.reason: Lacking documentation not completely submitted; Recommended JEV not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to ensure that prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF is included in the LDRRMFIP and integrated in the AIP to establish transparency and accountability of public funds.

We recommended that the management direct:
all heads of offices to strictly follow the cut-off dates during the year-end.
the City Accountant to see to it that the Financial statements will be submitted on time or on before February 14 of the ensuing year, as required in the above-mentioned laws.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately the subjected DVs.
Exert more effort to submit on time the Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls and Official Receipts including its supporting documents to the Office of the COA Auditor.
observations.reason: Check DVs for November and December 2022 were still very much delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to prioritize the conduct of meeting properly discussing the action(s) of the management regarding the matter. 

We also recommended to the management the creation of a Task Force Committee to:
recover the related documents;
Send communications to the persons accountable and inform them of their accountability as well as request for payment of the same. Otherwise, institute legal actions for nonfeasance; and
Closely coordinate with the Audit Team as to the result of the above recommendations.
observations.reason: Task Force has been created, however, no meetings conducted during the year to further the investigation of the “Deposits in Transit”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to direct the Accountant to:
thoroughly examine the individual subsidiary ledgers under the ‘Due to LGUs’ account and to transfer accounts that are not liabilities to other LGUs to the appropriate account, and
avoid using the Due to LGUs account for “special funds” and ensure that the collections and expenditures are properly recognized in the appropriate income and expense accounts.
observations.reason: “Due to LGUs” account still comprises sub-accounts that should be recorded as income as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Require the Accountant to ensure that expenditures are recorded as asset, expense, and/or liability in the year it was incurred, and
Direct the Budget Officer to observe the proper charging of expenditures in the appropriations as provided in the Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Still various previous years financial transactions were paid using CY 2021 funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Immediately implement COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances to be able to update the property records of the city and eliminate the significant amount “beginning balances” or for reconciliation, “constant” entries, as well as negative balances in the property records.
For account other than PPE, exert more efforts to retrieve the necessary records to properly identify the specific transactions in the lumped amount covered by entries with “constant” notations and thoroughly trace and analyze the abnormal balances in the subsidiary ledgers and determine the reason/s for any erroneous entry made to be able to make the necessary adjustment to correct the affected balances.
submit to the audit team copy of the JEV with supporting documents and working papers of all future adjustments related thereto.
observations.reason: Not adjusted in CY 2021 although few staff are working on the reconciliation as well as correction of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to implement COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with a reliable PPE balances to be presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Still no reconciliation in CY 2021, although few staff are, accordingly, working on the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the City Accountant to strictly adhere with the requirements on the submission of reports as required under the PRDP Financial Management Operations Guidelines.
observations.reason: Monthly trial balances and quarterly financial statements were still not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to require the City Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for the accounts maintained by LGU within the prescribed time mentioned in COA Circular No. 96-011.

We also recommended to the Management to enroll with the LandBank weAccess which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.
observations.reason: BRS for November and December 2021 were still very much delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Accountant to:
verify and validate all the transactions related to the receipts of performance security in the Trust Fund and reduce the same in a working paper with all the necessary data and information and supported with documents. Thereafter, make an adjusting entry to correct the misclassification and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers.
submit a written justification and supporting documentation relative to the insufficient performance security balance as of the end of December 31, 2020; containing therein his relative course of action and explanation as to why he should not be held responsible for his failure to exercise this oversight function.

We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the adjusting entries, together with the working papers and supporting documents to correct the misclassification and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers. Also, please submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation.

We recommended to the management to stop the payment of honoraria without legal basis and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the DBM implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160 and refrain from paying honoraria other than those enumerated under DBM Compensation Policy Guideline No. 98-1.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office must give time to verify each and every reconciling item in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and to effect all valid adjustments and corrections in the General Ledgers. 

We further recommended that reconciling items found during the preparation of the BRS should be immediately adjusted and corrected in the General Ledger through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.reason: The accounting Office still failed to verify the numerous reconciling items found in BRS.

observations.recommendation: Require the CGSO to conduct a complete physical count of PPE and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor. Advice the CAO to reconcile the PPE subsidiary records with that of the CGSO’s property cards based on the result of the count.
observations.reason: Still no reconciliation between the records of the CAO and the CGSO as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding CA.
observations.reason: Still have unliquidated cash advances as of Dec. 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: Require the CGSO to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City under his charge pursuant to Sections 375 & 490 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Titles were still not completely processed as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that AAR Management through the MSWDO, apprise the DSWD, RO No. VII of the eligible family members listed members as deceased, and recommend for possible replacement for identified the deceased replacement of beneficiaries. We also recommended that Management ensure systematic procedures while strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.
observations.managementAction: Management
observations.reason: Management was waiting for approval from the DSWD Central Office in Manila for the replacement for identified the deceased replacement of beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation for the erasures or alterations in the signature of distribution lists. We also recommended that Management avoid frequent erasures or alterations on the distribution lists to preserve its authenticity, and if it cannot be avoided, very few erasures may be allowed but should be countersigned.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 had submitted the justification of the distribution lists. We also recommended that Management avoid frequent erasures or alterations on the distribution lists to preserve its authenticity, and if it cannot be avoided, very few erasures may be allowed but should be countersigned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit (a) the Individual approved daily time record or the daily report of attendance of the 485 Municipal personnel paid with COVID-19 Hazard Pay showing the official dates and hours they physically reported for work at their respective offices or work stations during the implementation of the ECQ period; and (b) Copy of the approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 able to get a Certification in the workplace for the other entitlement personnel who were granted COVID-19 Hazard Pay.
observations.reason: The IATF Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace had also been submitted the IATF Resolution of the Risk Management Certification in the workplace for the Public Health Workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA to the nine PHWs: a) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of the Emerging Infectious Diseases Resolution of the ECQ; b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the SRA; c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.
observations.managementAction: The MHO was able to get a Certification from the Secretary of Health with the specified names of the PHWs.
observations.reason: The daily time records and documents showed that some of the data were not oriented and did not contain vital information such as Confirmed Case ID, Close Contact Name, Date of Last Exposure, and Date of Voluntary Quarantine Period Ends.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation for the unaccounted balance of four packs of milk. We also recommended that Management establish a validated list of affected beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 had already sent a letter to COA with the corrected distribution.
observations.reason: There was still no validated list of the affected beneficiaries used as basis for the distribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) establish a procedure for identifying PHWs who shall be entitled for the Hazard Pay set forth in the relevant guidelines; and b) The Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific hazardous/occupational risks with certification from appropriate agencies and other documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The MHO was partially able to get a Certification in the workplace from the Secretary of Health with the specified names of the PHWs.
observations.reason: The special order from the agency/department head did not specify the assignment to hazardous/difficult areas.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the rates of hazard pay per month applied to the monthly basic salary in accordance with Section 3.3 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016 dated July 15, 2016 subject to the Personal Services limitation in LGU budget, to ensure that health workers are justly compensated.
observations.managementAction: The Local Treasurer had already complied and implemented the recommended adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from making advance payments on government contracts unless specifically authorized by law or regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped making advance payments to the owners of properties to be acquired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board prepare their annual SEF budget based on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP. We also recommended that the pertinent provisions on the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board agreed to implement the recommendation starting CY 2021.
observations.reason: The recommendation was agreed to be implemented in January 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly utilize the LDRRMF for programs/projects/activities authorized under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and in accordance with DBM LBC No. 124 dated March 26, 2020. We also recommended that Management submit an explanation stating the reasons for the deficiencies: a) Provision of meals to PHWs who were receiving subsistence allowance; b) At least 19 frontliners were present and given meals in two checkpoints at the same time while at least 13 frontliners were given two meals at the same meal time in one and the same checkpoint; and c) Inconsistencies in name spellings, mismatched or different signature strokes and frequent erasures on dates and names in the distribution lists.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO submitted an explanation to the Auditor.
observations.reason: In CY 2021, expenditures amounting to ₱6,515,760.00 had been improperly charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by (a) conducting an intensive tax campaign in every barangay; (b) sending out notices of delinquencies; (c) posting a notice of the delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; and (d) publishing the notice of delinquency in a newspaper of general circulation. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in Section 256 of RA No. 7160 for collecting delinquent real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Treasurer, in collaboration with the Municipal Treasurer, had already drafted an Action Plan to put properties on sale through public auction in accordance with Section 256 of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: The said Action Plan was supposed to commence in the 1st quarter of CY 2020 but was deferred due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management cause the immediate completion of the project at no additional cost to the government so that it can be used for its intended purpose. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit proper accounting for the construction materials already procured but were not installed and desist from certifying as 100% completed any project unless he or his representative has conducted an inspection of the project.
observations.managementAction: The canopy along the western portion of the building and the roof framing works will continue as soon as COVID-19 pandemic is over.
observations.reason: At present, the Industrial Park is being used as a western quarantine facility and the engineering works will continue as soon as COVID-19 is over.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish copies of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to allow timely review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already submitted some Purchase Orders and contracts of some projects but beyond the reglementary period.
observations.reason: The contracts were submitted beyond the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Administrative Council, strictly adhere to the primary purpose of the technical and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality by only admitting out-of-school youths residing within the Municipality and thoroughly review the individual credentials of the applicants to determine if they are eligible to be admitted to the scholarship program. We finally recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation stating the reason(s) for a) admitting scholars who were neither out-of-school youths nor of school age; and b) accepting barangay certifications with altered dates of issue and submitting these as part of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already implemented adjustments and some scholars were college graduates.
observations.reason: There was no documentation for some of the scholars admitted who were neither out-of-school youths nor of school age.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective accomplished AAPSI on the CY 2018 AAR and assign a personnel for consolidation and submission to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Since CY 2018, the Municipality submitted AAPSI, and AAPSI for AAR 2018 was submitted on February 2, 2022.
observations.reason: The AAPSI for AAR 2018 was not submitted within the reglementary period; it was submitted only on February 2, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist establish the actual status of the breeding stock dispersal program by tracing the whereabouts and condition of the breeding stocks and render a report to the Municipal Accountant which includes the fair market value of each stock, for proper recording in the books of accounts. In case of death/loss of assets, necessary adjusting entries shall be taken only after the issuance of Credit Notice arising from the result of the evaluation of the relief from accountability, if any. We also recommended that proper disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with PPSAS 27.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist had submitted the property cards for the breeding stocks but had not updated the inventory report including the prevailing market price.
observations.reason: The property cards were submitted but the inventory report and prevailing market price were not regularly updated because the Livestock Inspector did not update the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the time deposit with the First Consolidated Bank, Dumaguete Branch to the Philippine Veterans Bank, Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160, DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, and COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Time deposit of ₱1,000,000.00 with FCB was transferred to the Philippine Veterans Bank on May 2, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the important PPAs such as the development and maintenance of GAD database, construction of Women in Crisis Center and the like, other than conduct of trainings/seminars/orientations.
observations.managementAction: The formulation of the GAD database is on-going. Programs/projects for CY 2021 had not been fully implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Programs/activities were not fully implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic; Municipality had not yet designated area for Women in Crisis Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take a concerted effort to completely establish the GAD database within CY 2019 in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: The formulation and maintenance of the GAD database is on-going. The Municipality started hiring new set of enumerators and field editors in early CY 2021 but had stopped due to the pandemic.
observations.reason: The GAD database was about 65% completed; efforts were halted due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of the delay. We also recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Personnel had been assigned to process the right-of-way acquisition and Barangay Officials were asked to coordinate with the concerned agencies.
observations.reason: The delay was due to the late acquisition of right-of-way, Deeds of Donation and appropriate documentation for construction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC allocate more funds to PPAs that are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned by RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Children's Profile had already been submitted; the Local Development Plan for Children was not yet prepared because it requires the local council to convene and approve the plan.
observations.reason: The Local Development Plan for Children had not yet been convened and approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and in the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: Operation on the open dumpsite had already been stopped per SB Resolution No. 30 dated February 4, 2020.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to fully implement the sanitary landfill due to insufficient funds; however, operations of the open dumpsite were ceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly allocate funds for the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and prioritize the implementation of programs and activities which would promote gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality assigned a GAD Coordinator to stop the use of GAD funds for scholarship program and to fast track implementation of GAD projects.
observations.reason: Some programmed GAD projects had not been implemented in CY 2021 due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the use of the three units cargo truck for ordinary, day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The cargo trucks had been used as transportation for delivery of necessary services such as health, education and construction of relevant facilities to remote areas, steep mountains and rough terrains.
observations.reason: The Municipality used the trucks for necessary services to remote areas due to lack of alternative transportation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant had already been maintaining the property records for each real property owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason for failing to fully implement the titling of municipal properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials implement the programs immediately and undertake a concerted effort to address whatever obstacles that may hinder the implementation and completion of the projects as scheduled. Thereafter, submit the necessary monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the project.
observations.managementAction: Operation on some projects already completed; unexpended balances not yet returned to source agencies.
observations.reason: Some unexpended balances were not yet returned to the source agencies; some projects were ongoing and some were completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately send demand letters to the beneficiaries with outstanding loans receivable of more than five years and monitor and deduct from the electric subsidy due to the beneficiaries to effect deduction from their outstanding EILP payable. Moreover, tax clearance was not issued if the clients were not cleared from the payment of water bill, real property tax, business tax and EILP payable pursuant to Municipal Ordinance No. 5, Series of 2015.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to beneficiaries in CY 2017; some beneficiaries can no longer be located.
observations.reason: Some beneficiaries can no longer be located; some beneficiaries were already contacted by barangay officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality purchased some supplies from PS-DBM; some purchases were done outside PS-DBM when items were not available or total cost less than ₱20,000.00.
observations.reason: Some supplies were procured outside PS-DBM because items were not available or total cost was below the threshold of ₱20,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee be required to complete the conduct of the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Property cards were maintained but reconciliation was not fully done; the stockroom did not have enough space to accommodate all items.
observations.reason: There was no full reconciliation between the physical count and the related property and accounting records; property cards were maintained but reconciliation incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU prepare and submit immediately to the Mayor and the Auditor the income statement and monthly balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, and thereafter, prepare and submit monthly financial reports; furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the operator to submit financial statements and monitoring reports; some financial statements were submitted annually instead of monthly.
observations.reason: There was partial submission of financial statements and the manager did not submit monthly monitoring reports as required by the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all items procured and designate a storekeeper; b) Materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged to facilitate retrieval and ensure that fresh usable items are always available; c) The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored; d) The Office of the municipal treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article; and e) Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality designated a stockroom and storekeeper in the Motorpool; the storekeeper was instructed to maintain a logbook and index cards.
observations.reason: The existing stockroom did not have enough space to accommodate all items procured by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference between the RPT and SET balances totaling ₱101,424,940.45 and, henceforth, conduct, together with the Municipal Assessor, a periodic reconciliation of the subsidiary balances of Basic and Special Education Tax dues and RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts to immediately detect and rectify any discrepancies; b) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and c) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for control accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: As of the present, the linkage of the iTax System is only between the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor. There is no linkage to the Accounting Office yet. Hence, MAO could not perform as yet due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by (a) conducting an intensive tax campaign in every barangay; (b) sending out notices of delinquencies; (c) posting a notice of the delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; and (d) publishing the notice of delinquent properties in a newspaper of general circulation. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in Section 256 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The said Action Plan was supposed to commence in the 1st quarter of CY 2020 but was deferred due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that detailed engineering is conducted before any POW is prepared and subsequently approved and to refrain from making revisions which are not necessary in order that projects will be completed on schedule, so that the desired benefits can be immediately enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries as well as reduce the risk that programmed funds may no longer be sufficient due to price increases in construction materials and labor or changes in economic conditions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason for non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer set a cut-off date for the submission of approved payrolls by the different Offices of the Municipality every other pay period, and require strict adherence thereto. We also recommended that the grant of multiple cash advances to DOs in one day be immediately stopped.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Verification of records revealed that in CY 2020, DOs were still granted multiple cash advances in one day for the payment of salaries and wages, honoraria and other similar payments. The Municipality has yet to set a cut-off date and strict adherence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist establish the actual status of the breeding stock dispersal program by tracing the whereabouts and regularly update the condition of the original breeding stocks and of their offspring, if any, and render a report to the Municipal Accountant which includes the fair market value of each stock, for proper recording in the book of accounts. In case of death/loss of assets, necessary adjusting entries shall be taken only after the issuance of Credit Notice arising from the result of the evaluation for the relief from accountability, if any. We also recommended that proper disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with PPSAS 27 to provide information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Work/Other Animals & Breeding Stocks account was not updated because of failure of the Livestock Inspector to update the account. Only the property cards were submitted by the Municipal Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of the delay. We further recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The delay was due to the late acquisition of road right-of-way, Deeds of Donation and Barangay officials were asked to coordinate with personnel to process the right-of-way acquisition.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

1. Exert efforts to collect the accounts receivable by sending demand letters to the concerned individuals/entity. 

2. Reclassify as non-current assets the portion of accounts receivable which do not conform to the criteria as provided in paragraph 76 of the IPSAS.
observations.reason: The same receivable that were found dormant in the previous year’s report still appeared in the aging of receivables as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert effort through the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the Accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had initially requested from the Department Heads the submission of the list of PPEs found in their respective premises as a guide in the conduct of the inventory count.
observations.reason: As December 31, 2020, depreciable assets that aggregated to ₽280,837,945.17 were not provided with depreciation contrary to the provision of Paragraph 43 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) No. 17.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to assign a Task Force Committee to: 
recover the related documents;
Send communications to the persons accountable and inform them of their accountability as well as request for payment of the same. Otherwise, institute legal actions for nonfeasance.
Closely coordinate with the Audit Team as to the result of the above recommendations.

For current deposits in transit, we recommended to the LCE to instruct the city accountant to make representations to the respective banks on the status of the deposits in transit.
observations.reason: Recommendations were not implemented in CY 2020. Reasons communicated to the audit team included the rise of the COVID-19 outbreak.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality, through the MSWDO, apprise
the DSWD, RO No. VII of the beneficiaries listed under the UCT program who
are already deceased, and recommend for possible replacement from among the
eligible family members of the deceased.

We also recommended that Management ensure systematic procedures
while strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation for the
erasures or alterations in the signature of 1,002 recipients contained in the
distribution lists. We further recommended that Management avoid frequent
erasures or alterations on the distribution lists to preserve its authenticity, and if
it cannot be avoided, very few erasures may be allowed but should be
countersigned.

We recommended and Management agreed that they will require the
personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the
Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required
information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated
August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the items above the
highest suggested retail price of the DOH. We further recommended that
Management procure medical supplies based on the SRP set by appropriate
government agencies to ensure that the Municipality get the most advantageous
prices.

We recommended that the following documents be immediately
submitted to the Auditor to determine compliance with the conditions on the
grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget
Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to wit:
a. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance;
b. Certificate of attendance during the ECQ period; and
c. IATF Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace
Likewise, we recommended that Management submit (a) Individual
approved daily time record or daily report of attendance of the 485 Municipal
personnel paid with COVID-19 Hazard Pay showing the official dates and hours
they physically reported for work at their respective offices or work stations
during the implementation of the ECQ period; and (b) Copy of the approved
contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health
Officer to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the
grant of SRA to the nine PHWs:
a) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious
Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace;
b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the
period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as
well as the entitlement of the SRA;
c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance
indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and
d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs
quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in
other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.

We recommended that Management ensure that donations in-kind and
relief goods are distributed to qualified beneficiaries based on a validated list of
affected individuals pursuant to Item II.2.b of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated
April 21, 2020. We also recommended that Management submit an explanation
for the unaccounted balance of four packs of milk.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to existing reporting
guidelines in order to provide timely and accurate data/information to
concerned office(s), particularly, the NDRRMC for it to be able to perform its
functions in managing disasters and allocating resources therefor.

We recommended that:
a. Management establish a procedure for identifying PHWs who shall be
exposed to high risk or low risk hazard including the duration of such
exposure duly approved by the Secretary of Health to support claims for
hazard pay; and
b. The Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs to support their claims for
hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and
occupational risks with certification from appropriate agencies and other
documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly with the rates of
hazard pay per month applied to the monthly basic salary in accordance with
Section 3.3 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016 dated July 15, 2016
subject to the Personal Services limitation in LGU budget, to ensure that health
workers are justly compensated.

We recommended that Management refrain from making advance
payments on government contracts unless specifically authorized by law or
regulations.

We recommended that the LSB prepare their annual SEF budget based
on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP. We also recommended that the
pertinent provisions on planning and budgeting for SEF under DepEd-DBM-
DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 be strictly followed.

We recommended that Management strictly utilize the LDRRMF for
programs/projects/activities authorized under DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and in accordance with DBM LBC
No. 124 dated March 26, 2020.

We also recommended that Management submit an explanation stating the
reasons for the following deficiencies:
d) Provision of meals to PHWs who were receiving subsistence
allowance;
e) At least 19 frontliners were present and given meals in two
checkpoints at the same time while at least 13 frontliners were given
two meals at the same meal time in one and the same checkpoint; and
f) Inconsistencies in name spellings, mismatched or different signature
strokes and frequent erasures on dates and names in the distribution
lists.

We recommended to the management to rectify, justify and/or submit the documents to substantiate all the deficiencies noted as well as submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation to conclude our audit on this matter.

We recommended to the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Discontinue recording collections received through banks as Cash-Local Treasury
Record subsequent disbursements of the SAP fund in the account “Donations.”
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to correct the erroneous entries and submit to the Office of the Auditor the working papers and other supporting documents.

We recommended to the management to:
instruct the City Accounting Office to submit the lacking payrolls to the Office of the Auditor; and
contact the beneficiary who received the assistance twice to refund amount equivalent to amount received by one beneficiary.
We also recommended that for future liquidations of the same nature, ensure complete submission of supporting documentations in order not to delay the conduct of post audit.

We recommended to the Management to -
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the adjusting entries, together with the working papers and supporting documents to correct the misclassifications and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers and submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation.;
Record subsequent receipts of the ER 1-94 fund in the account “Share from National Wealth.”
Instruct the City Budget Officer to include the balance of the ER 1-94 fund as source of fund for preparation of supplemental budget for the CY 2021 and make sure that the disbursements thereof are in accordance with Section 5.1 of DC No. 2020-040008 while the State of Public Health Emergency still exist. Receipts of ER 1-94 fund, once the health emergency is lifted, shall be considered in budget preparation and shall be guided by DC No. 2018-08-0021.
Instruct the City Treasurer, City Accountant, and City Budget Officer to coordinate with each other every time new fund is received by the LGU so that its nature can be properly determined in order to undertake necessary actions before, during, and after its disbursement; and
To submit explanation/justification for all the noted deficiencies as discussed above.

We recommended to the Management to direct the Accountant to:
Submit the lacking documentation to support the utilization of the above-cited disbursements,
Ensure that supplies and materials are recorded as inventory, and that the utilization are properly supported with complete documentation, and for construction materials to be supported with detailed plans and inspection reports after the completion of the project,
Prepare adjusting entries for the above-cited transactions that should be recorded as expense and inventory.
Record future disbursements of the same nature in an asset/inventory account.
We recommended to the Management to ensure that prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF is included in the LDRRMFIP and integrated in the AIP to establish transparency and accountability of public funds.

We recommended that the management direct:
all heads of offices to strictly follow the cut-off dates during the year-end.
the City Accountant to see to it that the Financial statements will be submitted on time or on before February 14 of the ensuing year, as required in the above-mentioned laws.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately the subjected DVs.
Exert more effort to submit on time the Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls and Official Receipts including its supporting documents to the Office of the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to prioritize the conduct of meeting properly discussing the action(s) of the management regarding the matter.
We also recommended to the management the creation of a Task Force Committee to:
recover the related documents;
Send communications to the persons accountable and inform them of their accountability as well as request for payment of the same. Otherwise, institute legal actions for nonfeasance; and
Closely coordinate with the Audit Team as to the result of the above recommendations.

We recommended to the Management to direct the Accountant to:
thoroughly examine the individual subsidiary ledgers under the ‘Due to LGUs’ account and to transfer accounts that are not liabilities to other LGUs to the appropriate account, and
avoid using the Due to LGUs account for “special funds” and ensure that the collections and expenditures are properly recognized in the appropriate income and expense accounts.

We recommended to the Management to:
Require the Accountant to ensure that expenditures are recorded as asset, expense, and/or liability in the year it was incurred, and
Direct the Budget Officer to observe the proper charging of expenditures in the appropriations as provided in the Local Government Code.

We recommended to the Management to:
Immediately implement COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances to be able to update the property records of the city and eliminate the significant amount “beginning balances” or for reconciliation, “constant” entries, as well as negative balances in the property records.
For account other than PPE, exert more efforts to retrieve the necessary records to properly identify the specific transactions in the lumped amount covered by entries with “constant” notations and thoroughly trace and analyze the abnormal balances in the subsidiary ledgers and determine the reason/s for any erroneous entry made to be able to make the necessary adjustment to correct the affected balances.
submit to the audit team copy of the JEV with supporting documents and working papers of all future adjustments related thereto.
We recommended to the management to implement COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with a reliable PPE balances to be presented in the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to require the City Accountant to strictly adhere with the requirements on the submission of reports as required under the PRDP Financial Management Operations Guidelines.

We recommended to the Management to require the City Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for the accounts maintained by LGU within the prescribed time mentioned in COA Circular No. 96-011.
We also recommended to the Management to enroll with the LandBank weAccess which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Accountant to:
verify and validate all the transactions related to the receipts of performance security in the Trust Fund and reduce the same in a working paper with all the necessary data and information and supported with documents. Thereafter, make an adjusting entry to correct the misclassification and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers.
submit a written justification and supporting documentation relative to the insufficient performance security balance as of the end of December 31, 2020; containing therein his relative course of action and explanation as to why he should not be held responsible for his failure to exercise this oversight function.

We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the adjusting entries, together with the working papers and supporting documents to correct the misclassification and post the same in the appropriate subsidiary ledgers. Also, please submit all the above required documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and validation.

We recommended to the management to stop the payment of honoraria without legal basis and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the DBM implementing the certain provisions of RA 7160 and refrain from paying honoraria other than those enumerated under DBM Compensation Policy Guideline No. 98-1.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate completion of the project at no additional cost to the government so that it can be used for its intended purpose. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit proper accounting for the construction materials already procured but were not installed and desist from certifying as 100% completed any project unless he or his representative has conducted an inspection of the project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: At present, the canopy along the western and southern portion of the building and the roof framing and roofing materials in the western portion were over. The engineering and roofing works will continue as soon as COVID-19 pandemic is over.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality furnish copies of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to allow timely review.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted copies of contracts and documents but not within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the effectivity of the respective fidelity bonds and expiration dates of AOs, and see to it that renewal thereof be made before their expiration dates. Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash advances granted to AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability covered by their fidelity bonds in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Records showed that in CY 2020, 21 bonded AOs, 18 AOs renewed their fidelity bond after the expiration dates. In fact, the late renewal was from one day to 39 days after expiration dates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality thru the Administrative Council of the technical and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality carry out their functions strictly in accordance with the Section 5 of Ordinance No. 5 dated August 5, 2019. We also recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation stating the reasons for screening, admitting and enrolling scholars in butchering/meat cutting/deboning course at BIBSCORP even before the Administrative Council was created.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: SB Resolution No. 355, series 2020 was approved on December 2, 2020 which carry out specified, among others, a. Composition of the Administrative Council; b. Functions of the Administrative Council; and c. Criteria and Guidelines for Admission to the Technical and Vocational Scholarship Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality thru the Administrative Council strictly adhere to the primary purpose of the technical and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality by granting scholarship only to out-of-school youths residing within the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 229, series of 2019. We further recommended that the Council thoroughly review the individual credentials of the applicants to determine if they are eligible to be admitted to the scholarship program. We finally recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation stating the reason(s) for a) admitting scholars who were neither out-of-school youths nor of school age; and b) accepting barangay certifications with altered dates of issue and submitting these as part of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The Municipality implemented the technical and vocational scholarship program but the scholars included college students. The Municipality stated that henceforth scholarship will be granted only to out-of-school youths. Some scholars were college students.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc.-Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The Municipality already made the payments when they received the audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective accomplished AAPSI on the CY 2018 AAR, and assign a personnel for consolidation and submission of the same to the Auditor within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Records showed AAPSI for AAR 2018 was duly submitted only on February 2, 2021. Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the incumbent Municipal officials and employees immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with the existing regulations. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Cash advances granted in CY 2020 were fully liquidated as of December 31, 2020.
