observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the time deposit with the First Consolidated Bank, Dumaguete Branch to the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160, DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, and COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Time deposits of ₱1,000,000.00 with the FCB were transferred with the FCB to the Philippine Veterans Bank on May 2, 2019 without stating the reason for doing so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the heads of offices be required to carefully plan the procurement of catering services for the various trainings, seminars, workshops, team building and sports-related activities to be conducted during the year. We also recommended that the BAC undertake the procurement through competitive bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR of RA No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: Starting from the 2nd semester of CY 2019, procurements of catering services were done through competitive bidding per activity, instead of consolidating them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years pursuant to Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We further recommended that the MDRRM Council take a concerted effort to plan and implement pre-disaster preparedness PPAs charged to prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The Municipality included in the procurement of welfare goods, drugs and medicines, and payment in response to the COVID-19 emergency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the important PPAs, such as the development and maintenance of GAD database, construction of Women in Crisis Center and the like, the conduct of trainings/seminars/orientations, to address gender issues. The municipality should ensure that PPAs related to GAD are implemented so that intended beneficiaries are assured of services, protection and other assistance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019
observations.reason: The formulation of the GAD database is on-going. The Municipality has not yet designated an area for the Women in Crisis Center, which needs proper structure planning from the GAD focal point system. Programs/activities for CY 2020 were not fully implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake the development and maintenance of GAD Database in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: The formulation of the GAD database is ongoing. Management started the hiring of new set of enumerators and field editors in the early part of CY 2020 but was stopped due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC allocate more funds to PPAs that are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned by RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The Children’s Profile was already prepared while the Local Development Plan for Children was not yet prepared because it requires the Local Council to convene and to plan. Some programs and activities were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and in the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: Operation on the open dumpsite was stopped. The Municipality purchased a Material Recovery Facility instead of opening and maintaining a sanitary landfill because they do not have enough funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly allocate funds for the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and prioritize the implementation of programs and activities which would promote gender equality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The Municipality programmed GAD projects; however, CY 2020 implementation was affected by COVID-19. Management assigned a GAD Coordinator to prioritize activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the use of the three cargo trucks for ordinary, day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The cargo trucks were used as transportation service for the safety of personnel assigned to deliver the necessary services, such as health, education, and construction of relevant facilities to remote areas, steep mountains and rough terrains.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the municipal properties. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016
observations.reason: The Local Chief Executive created a team from the Legal Office to take charge of the titling of municipal real properties. The Municipal Treasurer is maintaining property records for each real property but some properties are not yet titled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; b. Operate the Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same; and c. Conduct and hiring of personnel to manage the operation. The Municipality shall require the operator to submit to the Auditor the necessary inventory of stocks and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012
observations.reason: The operation on the Barangay Bagsakan project would entail possible expenses, especially in the construction of building, fixtures and storekeeping activities. The funds provided by DA was not sufficient for a profitable operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Prepare and submit immediately to the Mayor the income statement and monthly balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, and require the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary and required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project; b. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to the beneficiaries in CY 2017 thru Barangay Officials. The Manager of the Bagsakan did not submit monitoring reports including financial statements. Some required reports were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all items procured and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat; b. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged in such a way as to facilitate retrieval when needed and ensure that fresh usable items are always available. Materials should be issued on first in, first out basis; c. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored; d. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article. All receipts, issuances and balances on hand of supplies and materials should be kept up-to-date; and e. Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011
observations.reason: The Municipality designated a storekeeper in the Motorpool. The storekeeper was instructed to maintain a logbook and index card. The stockroom of the Municipality does not have enough space to accommodate all supplies procured by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management secure necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is any, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: No reason given for the failure to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee be required to complete the conduct of the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations. Conduct a reconciliation especially for newly acquired properties. Reconcile bin cards with the stock/property cards maintained by the stock clerk and with the physical count of stocks on hand; and conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Property cards were submitted and some physical counts were done. There was no complete reconciliation between the physical count and the related property and accounting records. The municipal treasurer’s office maintained some property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; operate the equipment and train its staff to manage the same; and require the operator to submit necessary inventory of stocks and records and the financial reports prepared and submitted every 5th of each month. The LGU should also prepare and submit the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012
observations.reason: The operation of the BB project would entail expenses; the funds provided by the DA was not sufficient. Some project activities were completed, and some unexpended balances were not yet returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Immediately prepare and submit to the Mayor the income statement and monthly balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, by requiring the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary monitoring and evaluation reports and reports on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project; b. Furnish the Auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011
observations.reason: Operation records: a demand letter was sent to beneficiaries. The Manager did not submit required monitoring and evaluation reports. Some financial and operational reports were not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all items procured and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat; b. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged in such a way as to facilitate retrieval when needed and ensure that fresh usable items are always available; c. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article; d. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article and keep all receipts, issuances and balances up-to-date; and e. Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011
observations.reason: A storekeeper was designated in the Motorpool and instructed to maintain logbook and index card. However, the Municipality's stockroom does not have enough space and complete system; reconciliation and full compliance remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV), for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher, taking into consideration the following adjusting entry:

Cash in Bank		₽82,117.35
	Accounts Payable	₽82,117.35
observations.managementAction: A JEV that indicated the adjustment for the stale checks was taken up in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Outstanding checks in the Bank Reconciliation Statements pertained only to checks issued in 2020 and have not become stale.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management remain compliant and adherent to tax laws to avoid the incurrence of penalties and charges.
observations.managementAction: Filing and payment of VAT and Income Tax for the Badiang Spring Resort and Hotel’s operation had already been undertaken in 2020.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management submit an explanation for the non-inclusion in its appropriation LCPC related projects, activities and programs.  Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA and refer to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for LCPC related programs, activities and projects which they could implement.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for the LCPC had already been included in the Annual Budget for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply with RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: Several LGU structures had insurance coverage in 2020.
observations.reason: Total expenses for insurance in CY 2020 amounted to ₽776,573.00

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.
observations.managementAction: Out of the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund, the LGU was able to realize the procurement of a rescue vehicle in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect from its households the applicable garbage fees, in accordance to Article F of the LGU’s Tax Ordinance No. 13-63.
observations.managementAction: Collection of garbage fees were made on a voluntary manner from the constituents who availed of the garbage collection services of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

1.  Management tap the assistance of barangay officials in ensuring that waste segregation is observed by its constituents. 

2.  Conduct activities to inform the constituents of their responsibility for waste reduction, recycling and composting, and for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer as the local waste management officer to monitor closely the implementation of the program among the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The ESWM in-charge stated that the LGU stopped the hiring of garbage segregators in 2019.   However, it was observed by the concerned officer that segregation of solid waste at the household level was not yet achieved.
observations.reason: Payment of wages included those of job order workers tasked in waste segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2012-2014 LDRRMF back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the local Sanggunian for other social services.
observations.managementAction: A large portion of the unexpended Calamity Funds accumulated from the previous years were utilized in the COVID-19 response.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₽4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

 Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₽4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The payroll maker, through the Municipal Accountant, was directed to submit a list of employees of the LGU whose take home pay fall below ₽5,000.00 for the proper guidance of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer who signed the Loan Certifications.
observations.reason: One employee had a take home pay which was below the minimum requirement of ₽5,000.00 in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management broaden its dissemination of the invitation to bid so that other willing suppliers would be made aware of the projects of the LGU.  Moreover, the management through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) have to welcome all capable bidders to be able to select the one which offers the most benefit to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Awarding of projects was undertaken even to a lone bidder provided that the bid submitted was responsive, as determined by the members of the Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 for the prevention of the incurrence of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The holding of various socio cultural activities in time with the fiesta celebration had been a practice of the LGU for a long time, not just to celebrate the occasion but to promote the town and its tourism potentials to visitors and balikbayans.
observations.reason: The activities relating to the celebration of the annual fiesta in 2020 were less than those of the preceding year, yet, a substantial amount of disbursements for the same celebration was incurred.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend management to see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be promptly and fully implemented as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated thereby improving the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of various projects under the Continuing Appropriation of the 20% Development Fund (from prior years) were pursued only in 2020. As for the current appropriation, however, more priority was given to expenditures that relate to COVID-19 response.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit, as soon as possible, its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation the AAR 2017. 

It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: Printed AAPSI for the CY 2019 AAR was submitted to COA by management. An online posting in the website was not yet accomplished because the LGU’s website development and design is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct:
The City Accountant to 1) promptly adjust reconciling items such as book errors, unrecorded interest income and bank charges in the books of accounts; 2) exert diligent effort to obtain from the bank debit and credit advices to support the debit and credit memos identified in the BRS that would also serve as bases in making the necessary adjustments in the books; 3) Compare the validated deposit slips against the bank statements and General Ledger of LCCA accounts to trace the unrecorded deposits; and 4) Compare the Accountant’s Advice duly acknowledged by the bank to the Report of Checks Issued submitted by the City Treasurer to ensure complete recording of check disbursements.
The City Treasurer to 1) ensure that all check disbursements are completely reported in the Report of Checks Issued and promptly submitted to the City Accountant for recording in the books; and 2)initiate the reconciliation of the cashbooks with SLs every end of the month or as the need arises.
observations.reason: Significant number of reconciling items included in previous findings still remain as of Sept. 2020 BRS (latest submission).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET balances totaling ₱101,424,940.45 and,
henceforth, conduct, together with the Municipal Assessor, a periodic
reconciliation of the balances of Basic and Special Education Tax dues and
RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts to immediately detect and
rectify any discrepancies;

b) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current
year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts
in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and

c) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for control accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of
delinquent real property taxes by (a) conducting an intensive tax campaign in
every barangay; (b) sending out notices of delinquencies; (c) posting a notice of
the delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in publicly
accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay; and (d) publishing the notice
of delinquency in a newspaper of general circulation. We further recommended
that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property
taxes provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate completion of
the project at no additional cost to the government so that it can be used for its
intended purpose. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer
submit proper accounting for the construction materials already procured but
were not installed and desist from certifying as 100% completed any project
unless he or his representative has conducted an inspection of the project.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to ensure that
detailed engineering is conducted before any POW is prepared and subsequently
approved to refrain from making revisions which are not necessary in order that
projects will be completed on schedule, so that the desired benefits can be
immediately enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries as well as reduce the risk that
programmed funds may no longer be sufficient due to price increases in
construction materials and labor or changes in economic conditions.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor
with copies of contracts together with the supporting documents within the
reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with
Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009,
to allow timely review.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as well as the
Accountable Officers monitor the effectivity and expiration dates of their
respective fidelity bonds and see to it that renewal thereof be made before their
expiration. Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform
their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash
advances granted to AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability covered by
their fidelity bonds in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.

We recommended and Mangement agreed that the irregular procedure in
the grant of cash advances, such as the grant of multiple cash advances to DOs in
one day be immediately stopped.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Treasurer set a cut-off date for the submission of approved payrolls by the
different Offices of the Municipality every pay period, and require strict
adherence thereto.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Administrative Council of the
technical and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality carry out their
functions strictly in accordance with Section 5 of Ordinance No. 5 dated August
5, 2019.

We also recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation
stating the reasons for screening, admitting and enrolling scholars in
butchering/meat cutting/deboning course at BIBSCORP even before the
Administrative Council was created.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality thru the
Administrative Council strictly adhere to the primary purpose of the technical
and vocational scholarship program of the Municipality by limiting the grant of
scholarship to out of school youths residing within the Municipality pursuant to
the provisions of Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 229, series of 2019.

We further recommended that the Council thoroughly review the
individual credentials of the applicants to determine if they are eligible to be
admitted to the scholarship program.

We finally recommended that Management submit a letter of explanation
stating the reason(s) for a) admitting scholars who were neither out-of-school
youths nor of school age; and b) accepting barangay certifications with altered
dates of issue and submitting these as part of the supporting documents to the
disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to require the
issuance of ORs from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental
Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We
further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the
leagues comply with this requirement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department
heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI on the CY
2018 AAR, and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the
Auditor. Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with the submission
of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.

We recommend  that  management  exert  effort  through  the  Municipal
Treasurer’s Office, to reconstruct  and  update  its  property  records  and
reconcile with the Accounting records  to  facilitate  the  setting  up  and
periodic provision of depreciation.

We recommend that management revert the  unexpended  balance  of
CYs 2012-2014 LDRRMF back to the unappropriated surplus of the General  Fund
for subsequent re-appropriation by the local  sanggunian  for  other  social
services.

We recommend that management consider  the  timely  implementation  of
projects lined up for  disaster  preparedness  to  better  prepare  for  and
effectively  mitigate  the  perilous  impact   of   natural   and   man-made
calamities.

We recommend that management ensure prompt and full implementation  of
the development projects as programmed in  order  to  achieve  the  intended
purpose which the fund was appropriated and improve the  socio-economic  and
environmental condition of the constituents.

We recommend that management:

      1. Exert efforts to collect the accounts receivable by sending  demand
letters to the concerned individuals/entity.

       2.  Reclassify  as  non-current  assets  the  portion   of   accounts
receivable which do not conform to the criteria as provided in paragraph  76
of the IPSAS.

We recommend that management remain compliant and adherent to tax
laws to avoid the incurrence of penalties and charges.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect
from its households the applicable garbage fees, in accordance to Article  F
of the LGU’s Tax Ordinance No. 13-63.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel
the unclaimed stale checks and furnish  the  accountant  with  the  original
Disbursement Vouchers  (DV),  for  the  preparation  of  the  Journal  Entry
Voucher, taking into consideration the following adjusting entry:
