observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC, in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial Plan, allocate more funds to PPAs that are responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as envisioned by RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted some LCPC programs and projects. The Local Development Plan for Children and the Children’s profile were prepared by the Local Council for the Protection of Children where the Council for Children needs to convene and plan.
observations.reason: The Local Council for the Protection of Children is a large book requiring the council to convene and plan; thus the plan was not fully prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from charging expenses not listed under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG related joint circulars to the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization of the SEF. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification that request for the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the provisions and the Municipal Accountant obtained necessary certifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: Operation on the open dumpsite was already stopped. The Municipality passed SB Resolution No. 30 dated February 4, 2020. The Municipality planned to establish a Material Recovery Facility instead of opening and maintaining a sanitary landfill because they do not have enough funds for the latter.
observations.reason: The Municipality lacked funds to establish a sanitary landfill; thus they opted for a Material Recovery Facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly allocate funds for the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and prioritize the implementation of programs and activities which would promote gender equality.
observations.managementAction: Management assigned a GAD Coordinator and implemented some GAD projects. For CY 2019, ₱2,026,261.71 or 19.31% out of total GAD Budget of ₱10,494,260.45 was utilized.
observations.reason: No proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management minimize the ordinary, day-to-day use of these vehicles in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for proper rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management made sure that these trucks were in excellent condition by providing regular maintenance services. The vehicles were used for necessary services such as health, education, and construction of relevant facilities in remote areas.
observations.reason: The use of military cargo trucks as service vehicles was necessary to ensure safety of Municipal personnel who facilitated the delivery of necessary services in remote mountainous and rough terrains.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; operate the Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same; and hire personnel to operate and manage the equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Cattle Fattening, Swine Breeding, and other projects were implemented. Funds provided by the DA were not sufficient for a profitable operation. The Municipality operated the projects and terminated the MOA where necessary.
observations.reason: Some unexpended balances were not yet returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU implement the projects immediately and undertake a concerted effort to address whatever obstacles that may hinder the implementation and completion of the project as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The projects were already implemented. Some beneficiaries can no longer be located. The Municipality made adjustments and completed the projects.
observations.reason: Some beneficiaries can no longer be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately send demand letters to the beneficiaries with outstanding loans receivable of more than five years pursuant to Section 3 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2001-3. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct from the electric subsidy due the beneficiaries if they still are unable to pay.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to beneficiaries. Certain amounts were deducted from the electric subsidy of beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Some of the beneficiaries can no longer be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and comply with Administrative Order No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality procured some supplies from PS-DBM and other supplies outside PS-DBM when items were not available or total cost was less than ₱20,000.00.
observations.reason: Some supplies were not available from PS-DBM or the total cost was less than ₱20,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management secure necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted documents and requested necessary deeds where applicable.
observations.reason: Documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the inventory Committee be required to complete the conduct of the physical count of the properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was ongoing. Designated storekeeper was instructed to maintain a logbook and index card. Some offices were late in submission as of CY 2019 due to renewal of business permits.
observations.reason: Delay in the submission of inventory of PPEs assigned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU terminate the MOA and operate the Barangay Bagsakan; require the operator to submit the income statement and monthly balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan as of December 31, 2012 and require the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary reports including data/information and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: The operator of the BB project was already informed of the required submission of financial statements and monitoring reports. The Manager of the Bagsakan failed to provide the monitoring and evaluation reports and marketing strategies. The LGU sent a demand letter and required submission of reports.
observations.reason: There was partial submission of the required reports; the manager of the Bagsakan failed to provide the monitoring and evaluation reports and marketing strategies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all supplies and materials and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat. b. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged in such a way as to facilitate retrieval when needed and ensure that fresh usable items are always available. Materials should be issued on first in, first out basis; c. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored. Reconcile bin cards with the stock/property cards maintained by the stock clerk and with the physical count of stocks on hand; d. The Office of the municipal treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article. All receipts, issuances and balances on hand of supplies and materials should be kept up-to-date. e. Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: Designated a storekeeper in the Motorpool. The storekeeper was instructed to maintain a logbook and index card. Some offices of the Municipality were assigned to maintain their respective inventories. Physical count was conducted.
observations.reason: The Municipality lacked personnel who can handle the various tasks; implementation ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:  

1. The Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to the supplier the applicable liquidated damages arising from the delay in the implementation of the aforementioned projects.

2. Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer effect constant monitoring of projects and if warranted, the procuring entity may rescind awarded contracts.
observations.managementAction: Final payment for the completed projects was claimed by the contractor. It was deducted with liquidated damages as determined by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer.
observations.reason: Payment to the supplier was net of liquidated damages.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant instructed the concerned personnel to follow the regular course of processes in procuring goods and services as procurement by reimbursement was not provided in RA 9184.
observations.reason: The observation pertained to the procedures for the procurement of goods and services for the Badiang Resort and Hotel. In 2018, procurements (except from the revolving fund receipts) were no longer undertaken by means of reimbursement.

observations.managementAction: The formulation of the terms and conditions in relation to availing the goods and services offered by the Badiang Spring Resort and Hotel was undertaken in 2019.
observations.reason: Long overdue accounts receivable from the customers remained uncollected and unsettled as of 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

1.  Management tap the assistance of barangay officials in ensuring that waste segregation is observed by its constituents. 

2.  Conduct activities to inform the constituents of their responsibility for waste reduction, recycling and composting, and for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer as the local waste management officer to monitor closely the implementation of the program among the barangays.
observations.managementAction:
The disbursements of general services under the Ecological Solid Waste Management (ESWM) pertained to the wages of the dump truck drivers and garbage collectors.  

The ESWM in-charge stated that the LGU stopped the hiring of garbage segregators in 2019.   However, it was observed by the concerned officer that segregation of solid waste at the household level was not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₽4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₽4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The payroll maker, through the Municipal Accountant, was directed to submit a list of employees of the LGU whose take home pay fall below ₽5,000.00 for the proper guidance of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer who signed the Loan Certifications.
observations.reason: One employee had a take home pay which was below the minimum requirement of ₽5,000.00 in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply with RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: The Badiang Spring Hotel building and the municipal hall had insurance coverage in 2019. All the other structures however are yet to be insured.
observations.reason: Copies of insurance policies submitted by management to this Office showed that selected properties of the LGU were insured in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management broaden its dissemination of the invitation to bid so that other willing suppliers would be made aware of the projects of the LGU.  Moreover, the management through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) have to welcome all capable bidders to be able to select the one which offers the most benefit to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Awarding of projects was undertaken even to a lone bidder provided that the bid submitted was responsive, as determined by the members of the Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 for the prevention of the incurrence of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The holding of various socio cultural activities in time with the fiesta celebration had been the long practice of the LGU not just to celebrate the occasion but to promote the town and its tourism potentials to visitors and balikbayans.
observations.reason: Although the activities relating to the celebration of the annual fiesta in 2018 were not as many as those with   its preceding year, still a substantial amount of disbursements for the same celebration were incurred.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend management to see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be promptly and fully implemented as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated thereby improving the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Due to the receipt of funds from different national agencies for the previous years, the LGU decided to defer the implementation of the programmed project with its own funding to give priority to the immediate implementation of the projects and programs funded by the national government agencies.
observations.reason:
Few projects funded under the prior years’ continuing appropriation of the 20% Development Fund were implemented in 2019.  The LGU prioritized the implementation of high valued projects funded by the national government agencies.

This observation is reiterated in the 2019 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of DF projects under the 20% Development Fund (DF) by addressing the causes of the delay. We further recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.
observations.reason: The deed of donation or the land titles of the project sites were not yet secured. There was also an issue regarding the transport of materials to the project site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Prepare and submit immediately to the auditor the income statement and balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, by requiring the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary data/information and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month. b. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.reason: No financial statements were submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management submit an explanation for the non-inclusion in its appropriation LCPC related projects, activities and programs.  Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA and refer to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for LCPC related programs, activities and projects which they could implement.
observations.managementAction: The specified budgetary requirement provided in DILG MC No. 2012-120 has been disseminated only after the preparation of the CY 2017 Annual Budget. Thus no allocation was provided in the Annual Budget of the said year.
observations.reason: The CY 2018 Annual Budget did not provide an appropriation for the LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to conduct inventory of insurable properties and submit listing to GSIS for evaluation/appraisal of the insurance coverage and comply with RA 656 and Memorandum Circular No. 634 dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is yet to create an Inventory Committee as well as an Appraisal Committee to undertake the physical count of all properties of the LGU and perform the steps necessary for the appraisal of the properties. However, only the Badiang Resort and Hotel building has been provided with appropriation for property insurance as of CY 2018.
observations.reason: No disbursement for the insurance of buildings was recorded in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

a)	Exert effort through the General Services Office, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the Accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation. 

b)	Direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the policies and guidelines of Section 4(o) of NGAS Vol. I and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to arrive at an accurate computation for a fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The provision of depreciation cannot be fully implemented in so far as to PPEs, which the Municipal Accountant is doubtful as to its physical existence as no inventory taking is made and no inventory report is prepared by the General Services Office.
observations.reason: The observed deficiency is reiterated in this year’s (2018) AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough
verification and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts and provide for an
allowance for impairment in accordance with the PPSAS or PFRS.

We recommended and the incumbent Municipal officials and employees
agreed to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance
with the existing regulations. We further recommended and the Municipal
Mayor, likewise, agreed to file the request for authority to write-off dormant
unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or
Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures provided under COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist establish the actual
status of the breeding stock dispersal program by tracing the whereabouts and
condition of the original breeding stocks and their offspring, if any, and render a
report to the Municipal Accountant which includes the fair market value of each
stock, for proper recording in the book of accounts.

In case of death/loss of assets, necessary adjusting entries shall be taken
only after the issuance of Credit Notice arising from the result of the evaluation
of the relief from accountability, if any.

We also recommended that proper disclosure shall be provided in the
Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with PPSAS 27 to provide
information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the time deposit
with the First Consolidated Bank, Dumaguete Branch to either Land Bank of the
Philippines, Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines,
Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160, DOF
Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, and COA Circular No.
92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the heads of offices be required to carefully plan
the procurement of catering services for the various trainings, seminars,
workshops, team building and sports-related activities to be conducted during
the year. We further recommended that the BAC undertake the procurement
through competitive bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR
of RA No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately stop the
payment of the employer’s share of Pag-IBIG Fund premiums in excess of what
is required by law, which is only two percent of the maximum monthly
compensation of ₱5,000.00 or ₱100.00 per employee.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRM Council
include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF
of the previous years pursuant to Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We further recommended that the
MDRRM Council take a concerted effort to plan and implement pre-disaster
preparedness PPAs charged to prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management implement the important PPAs,
such as the development and maintenance of GAD database, construction of
Women in Crisis Center and the like, to address gender issues, other than the
conduct of trainings/seminars/orientations.

We recommended that Management take a concerted effort to completely
establish within CY 2019 the GAD database storage of necessary information in
aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to
ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the
intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other
assistance that may be provided.

We recommend that:
1. The Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to the supplier the applicable liquidated damages arising from the delay in the implementation of the aforementioned projects.
2. Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer effect constant monitoring of projects and if warranted, the procuring entity may rescind awarded contracts.

We recommend that management:
Exert effort through the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the Accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the policies and guidelines of Section 4(o) of NGAS Vol. I and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to arrive at an accurate computation for a fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommend that the management broaden its dissemination of the invitation to bid so that other willing suppliers would be made aware of the projects of the LGU.  Moreover, the management through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) have to welcome all capable bidders to be able to select the one which offers the most benefit to the LGU.

We recommend that management:
1. Send billing statement to the concerned persons and entity with outstanding accounts at the Badiang Spring Resort and Hotel.
2. Stop the practice of indiscriminately granting credit lines to any LGU official or employee. Those that are allowed with shall be subjected to credit terms and conditions.

We recommend that management revert the unexpended balance of CYs 2012-2013 LDRRMF back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the local sanggunian for other social services.

We recommend that management:
1. Exert efforts to collect the accounts receivable by sending demand letters to the concerned individuals/entity.
2. Make representations to the BQ Gases and BOHECO II for the possible withdrawal of deposits paid by the municipality.
3. Reclassify as non-current assets the portion of accounts receivable which do not conform to the criteria as provided in paragraph 76 of the IPSAS.
We recommend that management keep up the good work and employ continuous efforts to improve the financial performance of its waterworks system, public market and the LGU as a whole.

We recommend that:
1.  Management tap the assistance of barangay officials in ensuring that waste segregation is observed by its constituents.
2.  Conduct activities to inform the constituents of their responsibility for waste reduction, recycling and composting, and for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer as the local waste management officer to monitor closely the implementation of the program among the barangays.

It is recommended that management submit an explanation for the non-inclusion in its appropriation LCPC related projects, activities and programs.  Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA and refer to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for LCPC related programs, activities and projects which they could implement.

We recommended that effective immediately all cash advances of the Disbursing Officers must be supported with all the necessary documents enumerated in COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to validate the accuracy of the amount of the cash advance and minimize excess cash advances.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office shall update its accounting records relative to all loans availed by the LGU of Valencia City and record the same to its respective SL. Thereafter, the said SL must be well monitored and maintained.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office shall work back on the debit and credit entries made in the said SLs, prepare the corresponding working papers; and once validated and confirmed, make the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments. Any over remittance attributable to an employee shall be refunded by the latter.
We further recommended that the City Accounting Office must closely monitor the remittances of all kinds of payroll deductions that the same must be matched to each other.

We recommended that the transfer of funds from TF and SEF to GF for the EWT and EVAT must be done first before remitting the same to the BIR.
We further recommended that the said transfer must be supported with working papers showing the details and all other information/references in order to avoid discrepancies and error both in recording the same in the SLs and remittances to the BIR.

We recommended that management will review each and every account covered (70 accounts) in order to identify the accounts that are supposed to be part of the general income and thereafter, transfer the same to the general income of the city. This will form part of the surplus, if warranted, subject to an appropriation.
We further recommended that those accounts that are only entrusted to the City must be transferred to the Trust Fund.
Furthermore, we recommended that that accounts that are not supposedly recorded under the account “Due to LGUs” must be transferred to the correct and proper account.
Likewise, please submit to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Vouchers together with its supporting documents including the working papers for the transfers of accounts to its proper account.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office facilitates the transfer of the said general income from trust fund to general fund of the city. This will form part of the surplus, and if warranted, can be appropriated.
Likewise, please submit to the Office of the Auditor the Journal Entry Voucher together with its supporting documents including the working papers for the said transfer.

We recommended that the disbursements/payments made for the four (4) subject on-going infrastructure projects shall be transferred to the proper account (CIP) through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher with supporting working papers of complete information and details.
We further recommended that thereafter, the account “CIP” must be used as a debit entry for all on-going projects. The account “Due to NGA” shall only be closed upon completion of the projects.

We recommended that the agency must adhere to the procedures and documentations on the grant of extensions of contract time and the same must be approved by the agency head.
We further recommended that the concerned contractors must be charged with liquidated damages (LD); and the basis of the computation of the LD for those already charged with LD must be reviewed and corrected, if warranted.
Moreover, please furnish the Office of the Auditor the photocopies of the official receipts representing the payments for the said LD and/or additional LD.

We recommended that in the mean-time the City Accounting Office shall work back with all of the on-going projects which are implemented thru straight contracts and make the appropriate SLs which will reflect the correct entries and amounts.
We further recommended to implement the same in the subsequent projects.

We recommended that the valid and legal expenses chargeable against the allotment for pre-engineering and project monitoring in all infrastructure projects of the agency must be in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations. It should not be obligated and temporarily kept under a liability account without bonafide obligations.
We further recommended that the accumulated amount of P10,109,525.76 as of November 2018 and any additional of similar transactions in December 2018, must be reverted to unappropriated surplus or as one of the sources of funds for a supplemental appropriation of the City.

We recommended that the Statement of Account shall be supported with the actual date, time and number of hours of equipment usage duly signed by the equipment operator and shall be validated by the barangay officials concerned.
We further recommended that an inspection of the barangay road maintenance project shall be done and acceptance thereof by the barangay officials concerned.
We recommended that the procurement of POL must be done in accordance with the procurement law.

We recommended that the agency must adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 particularly on the rules and regulations on the alternative mode of procurement of shopping and small value procurement.
We further recommended that RFQ and corresponding Abstract of Quotations must be prepared for all procurement of goods.

We recommended that management must abide by the rules and regulations mentioned in the procurement law regarding refunds of bidding documents fee.
We further recommended that management should submit justifications for each of the refunds made of the bidding document fees as stated in the above matrix, otherwise, refund the same.

We recommended that the purchase of rice in bulk must be treated as inventory and consumption thereof must be supported with approved issuance slips where the name of recipients and signatures must be affixed.

We recommended that the LGU of Valencia City must improve and implement 100% the monitoring of dispensing fuel consumption.
We further recommended that: 1) all withdrawals from the identified gasoline station must be supported with trip tickets and the same must be completely filled-up, 2) signatures of the official driver, official passengers and approving officer must be affixed; and 3) the Plate No. of the vehicle and purpose must be written. 4) A “Report of Fuel Consumption” must be prepared for fuels used for heavy equipment and said report must bear the signatures of the concerned officials in charge.
Furthermore, to improve the existing monitoring of dispensing fuel consumption, we suggest that a pre-numbered withdrawal slip stating the number of liters, the signatures of the requesting party and approving officer must be affixed, and purpose must be stated clearly. This will be presented upon withdrawal in the gasoline station together with the trip ticket.
We recommended that adjusting entries in the Books of Accounts of the City of Valencia must always be well justified and supported with sufficient documents to validate the same.
We recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.reason: Per RAO, only 34.47% of CY 2018 Appropriation was utilized. The development of the children’s Comprehensive Rehabilitation Program for Child Protection Unit as of was on-going while the repair and maintenance of Child Protection Unit were not implemented because these are located away from the DSWD Office. Also, the Procurement Section refused to accept the activity designs for the feeding program because these were submitted by the concerned principals only in December 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LSB refrain from charging the SEF expenses not listed under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant certification that request for the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council.
observations.managementAction: POWs were prepared before the commencement of any project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare the POW with detailed engineering before prosecuting any project in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the same is properly carried out to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.reason: POWs were prepared before commencement of any project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately transfer the bags of cement to a secured place to prevent further damage and loss; account for all deliveries and withdrawals of the bags of cement; maintain property cards and submit a copy of the inventory report to the Auditor; and determine the number of bags of cement stolen or damaged, identify the personnel liable therefor and require them to pay for the money value thereof.
observations.reason: The bags of cement are now stored in the bodega at the back of the Municipal Building. Also, the Municipal Engineering Office coordinated with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office regarding the safekeeping of the bags of cement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still operating its open dumpsite because they do not have enough funds to open and maintain a sanitary landfill. However, they budgeted for the purchase of a Material Recovery Facility (MRF) which is already due for bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the installation of signboards in the project sites to provide the public information and guidance about the on-going projects being undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: Signboards were already installed in the project sites.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to properly allocate funds for the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We further recommended that the Municipality stop the use of GAD funds for scholarship program and, henceforth, prioritize the implementation of programs and activities which would promote gender equality.
observations.reason: 5% of the CY 2018 budget for GAD was appropriated. The scholarship program was still included in the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the use of the three units cargo truck for ordinary, day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.
observations.reason: The cargo trucks were still used in ordinary, day-to-day operations of the Municipality as they do not have other available motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of applications for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.
observations.reason: There was a need to designate a personnel to work on the processing of the documentary requirements pursuant to DENR AO No. 2016-21.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials implement the programs immediately and undertake a concerted effort to address whatever obstacles that may hinder the implementation and completion of the project as scheduled.
observations.reason: The Cattle Fattening and Swine Breeding Programs were completed. The implementation of the Local Tourism Development Projects were on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately send demand letters to the beneficiaries with outstanding loans receivable of more than five years. Payment of EILP loan was required by the MTO before the issuance of tax clearance.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to the beneficiaries. Payment of outstanding loan was required as per Municipal Ordinance before the issuance of tax clearance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately issue demand letters to the accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances. Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of advances to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated amounts and to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers were no longer connected with the Municipality or were transferred to other offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the PS-DBM provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.reason: It was more economical to purchase common-use supplies outside considering the expenses for transportation, labor, and per diems of personnel in picking up the items. Lack of personnel also contributed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management secure the necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is any, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.reason: There was a need to designate a focal person to negotiate and work on the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee be required to complete the conduct of the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The required physical count was already conducted.
observations.reason: The inventory count did not reconcile with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that the Accountant of the Municipality of Valencia exerts efforts to trace the long outstanding Due from LGU account, and cause the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
observations.reason: The Accountant was still waiting for the Mayor’s authority to write off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets. b. Require that subject properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be transferred immediately to the General Fund. c. Require the LGU to maintain properly accomplished/update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: The physical count was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Cooperative; b. Operate the concerned Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same.
observations.reason: The amount is only minimal and the Operator of the Barangay Bagsakan is still operating under the management of the Valencia Community Cooperative but the Municipality coordinated to improve operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist regularly report to and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant any addition to or reduction from the Work/Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Accounts. We likewise recommended for the conduct of a physical count of the livestock to determine their existence which shall be reconciled with the Livestock Ledger Card pursuant to Section 442 of the GAAM, Volume II. We further recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist or concerned official requests for relief of accountability for dead animals and when granted, the same may be dropped from the books of accounts in order to present fairly the asset account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Inventory of breeding stocks and other biological assets was not submitted by the Municipal Agriculturist to the Municipal Accountant; physical count was not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality maintain a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all supplies and materials and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat. The stockroom should be secured against pilferage and protected from deteriorating effects of the elements; b. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged to facilitate retrieval and ensure that fresh usable items are always available. Materials should be issued on first in, first out basis; c. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored; reconcile bin cards with the stock/property cards maintained by the stock clerk and with the physical count of stocks on hand; d. The Office of the municipal treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article; and e. Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit the inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The required inventory count was conducted and the stockroom logbook and index cards were instructed to be maintained.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel. Some storekeeping functions were assigned and reconciliation efforts were undertaken; however, complete implementation remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that, henceforth, management require from the job order and contractual personnel the submission of accomplishment report every month and to direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to indicate in the contract of service the specific job description or work to be performed by the job order and contractual personnel.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO has already provided in the Job Order or Contract of Service the specific job description of each hired personnel as well as the permanent employees and those hired under casual plantilla employment are required to indicate their respective work accomplishment on a daily basis in their Daily Time Record retrieved from the Biometrics System.
observations.reason: Payrolls for wages and honorarium paid to job order employees and contractuals, respectively, were supported with accomplishment reports covering the particular pay period.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant instructed the concerned personnel to follow the regular course of processes in procuring goods and services as procurement by reimbursement was not provided in RA 9184.
observations.reason: The observation pertained to the procedures for the procurement of goods and services for the Badiang Resort and Hotel. In 2018, procurements (except from the revolving fund receipts) were no longer undertaken by means of reimbursement.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:

1.	Adhere to the provisions of Section 3G02 and 3G03 of the Revenue Code of Valencia so as to discourage delayed payment of water bills, minimize, if not eliminate delinquencies and realize the income due to the government for a particular period.  The Valencia Waterworks System incharge shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in this regard.

2.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect from the delinquent water consumers the amount due to the LGU pertaining to the uncollected income due to unimposed penalties. 

3.	Issue Notices of Disconnection for delinquent accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Although the penalties collected cannot be accurately determined from the collections for the reason that these were receipted in total with the water bill collected, the Waterworks System collector maintained that penalties were billed to the concerned customer.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer in charge of the Waterworks System operation are instructed to coordinate for the matter of the issuance of Notices of Disconnection and the subsequent disconnection, if applicable.
observations.reason: Increase in revenue generated from the CY 2018 operation of the LGU’s Waterworks System compared to that in CY 2017 is noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO  to submit  the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and  Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: A quarterly report on the Utilization of the DRRMF prepared by the Municipal Accountant is submitted to the DILG  as one of the reports to be posted in its portal as required in Financial Disclosure Reporting.
observations.reason: The subject reports were submitted to the concerned government agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials and employees plan and incur travel to only those which are urgent and extremely necessary and will involve the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: All department heads are already reminded to minimize the incurrence of travel expenses and as much as possible allow only one personnel to travel during a certain date for all errands of the office unless the concerns or matters to attend to in various government offices would require otherwise.
observations.reason: Travelling expenses amounting to             ₽1,500,720.63 was lesser in CY 2018 compared to its preceding year which totaled ₽1,512,871.81.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₽4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₽4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The payroll maker, through the Municipal Accountant, was directed to submit a list of employees of the LGU whose take home pay fall below ₽4,000.00 for the proper guidance of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer who signed the Loan Certifications.
observations.reason: A decrease in the number of employees whose net take home were below ₽4,000.00 was noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 for the prevention of the incurrence of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The holding of various socio cultural activities in time with the fiesta celebration had been the long practice of the LGU not just to celebrate the occasion but to promote the town and its tourism potentials to visitors and balikbayans.
observations.reason: Although the activities relating to the celebration of the annual fiesta in 2018 were not as many as those with   its preceding year, still substantial amount of disbursements for the same celebration were incurred.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend management to see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be promptly and fully implemented as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated thereby improving the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Due to the receipt of funds from the different national agencies for the previous years, the LGU tends to defer the implementation of the programmed project with its own funding to give priority to the immediate implementation of the projects and programs funded by the national government agencies.
observations.reason: Projects funded under the continuing appropriation of the 20% Development Fund were implemented in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness and mainstream these in their activities to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.
observations.managementAction: The LDRMO has already been informed of this concern and answered that because he is aware that the amount appropriated shall be treated as continuing appropriation and can still be implemented in the succeeding year, he prioritized the training programs of his office instead for capacitating the Disaster Response Team.
observations.reason: The two major project being mentioned was subjected for a public bidding in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Subsidiary Ledger (SL) of all cash advances must be maintained for each officer and employee of the City of Valencia. The said SL must contain all the details of the cash advance such as the date granted, check number, fund, and brief description of the nature of the cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office assigned a specific accounting personnel (a CPA) to establish the SLs for all kinds of cash advances. As of the end of December 2018, there were still minimal adjustments to be made in order to have an accurate data in the SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office, particularly the collectors, must follow the procedures in accepting payment by checks as stipulated in Book Two, Chapter 2, Section 57 (G) of the Local Treasury Operation Manual and the City Treasurer or his designated Liquidating Officer shall verify the Report of Collections and Deposits
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer installed control measures in compliance to the audit recommend-ation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all collectors must provide a column for running balances representing the difference between the debit/credit columns for each fund. 
	We further recommended that the RCDs must be prepared with extra care, checked and verified before submitting the same to the City Accounting Office for JEV preparation.
	Moreover, we recommended that the Accountable Forms both the receipts, issuances and balances must be checked and verified.
observations.managementAction: There were still cashbooks and RCD, particularly of the city collectors, which were not properly presented/ recorded/maintained as observed during the cash examination conducted in July 2018.
observations.reason: Lacks close monitoring

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office shall maintain/prepare Cashbook - Cash In Treasury for collections and deposits under “Trust fund” and cash in bank under “LBP Account No. 0352-1140-22” immediately after the cash examination or effective August 1, 2017.

	Also, all kinds of collections and deposits must be completely recorded in the cashbook for easy reference and monitoring.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office must review or revisit the provisions or the accounting rules and regulations on how to account for cash disbursements both from regular and special cash advances.

	We further recommended that the City Accounting Office must monitor the liquidations of all cash advances granted to special disbursing officers.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office must give time to verify each and every reconciling item in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and to effect all valid adjustments and corrections in the General Ledgers.
	We further recommended that reconciling items found during the preparation of the BRS should be immediately adjusted and corrected in the General Ledger through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office did not create a team or assigned personnel to do the task exclusively.
observations.reason: The City Accounting Office failed to give time to verify the numerous reconciling items as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the preparation of the payroll for claims of BAC honorarium must be in accordance with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular mentioned above and the supporting documents to be attached to the payroll must be in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-001 as mentioned above also.
	We further recommended for the submission of the documentary requirements for us to complete our post audit.
We recommended that management must abide with the provision of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 as to sources of funds for payment of BAC honorarium to avoid incurring irregular transactions.

We recommended that management shall abide with the provision stipulated in Section 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the documentary requirements in availing communication expenses. 
We further recommended that management shall abide with the provisions stated in  Annex D (5) and Annex F (1) of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid excessive and unconscionable expenditures of government funds. 
Likewise, please submit to the Office of the Auditor the required documents for the claimed communication allowances.

We recommended that the lacking relevant documents to support the payrolls of the supplemental feeding program as enumerated in the table above shall be submitted to the City Accounting Office for checking and verification and the same shall be forwarded to the Office of the Auditor for post audit purposes.
	We further recommended that thereafter the disbursements for supplemental feeding program must be sufficiently supported with documents. 
	Moreover, we recommended for the submission of the documents to support the difference as shown in column 9 of the table above or refund the same.

We recommended that the designated focal person for every project/program funded by other government departments and offices must coordinate with the City Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office in order that receipts of trust funds can be accurately monitored. 
We further recommended that the City Accounting Office must always be furnished with the MOA and the corresponding WFP for each and every project/program in order to serve as the guidelines in the dispensing of funds and provide all the necessary information needed in the recording of the same particularly in the subsidiary ledgers.
We recommended that management to meticulously screen expenses charged against the Special Education Fund in order to maximize the utilization of the SEF for its purpose.

We recommended that all disbursement of funds under the SEF must be certified by the city superintendent of school as to its validity, propriety and legality before processing the same for the approval of the City Mayor (as the co-chairman of the LSB) and any payment can be made.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursements bear no signatures of the city superintendent/district supervisor
observations.reason: Laxity of manage-ment

observations.recommendation: We recommended that recording of receipt of the share of the National Wealth must be on a cash basis as prescribed in the NGAS.
observations.managementAction:
The share from the national wealth which were received in the CY 2018 were still recorded under the trust liability account. 
Part of audit finding no. 5      of this audit report.

Although, the share from the national wealth form CY 2017 backward were already treated as income in CY 2017.
observations.reason: The City Accounting Office seemed reluctant to the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, particularly the BAC Secretariat, should submit the purchase orders and contracts; and each and every supporting document related to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects of the LGU, complete and not on installment basis, in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Sec. 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The management appoint a new head for the BAC Secretariat
observations.reason: Still with minimal de-ficiencies noted

observations.recommendation:
• Immediately stop the current practice in recording transactions involving petty cash fund and strictly follow the prescribed imprest system;
• Ensure that sufficient internal control is in place by requiring quarterly reconciliation of the Petty cash book and petty cash on hand, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We have recommended that the Management should strictly follow the foregoing provisions in complying the necessary documentary requirements in processing payments or disbursements of financial assistance to NGOs.

a.	Strictly observe the actual delivery dates in comparison to the delivery period and deduct liquidating damages immediately if deem necessary especially when it is apparent that the supplier incurs delays upon delivery in conformity to the specific provisions cited in the General Conditions of a Contract;

We recommended that the LCE initiate the total implementation of the prioritized projects funded from the 20% LDF.  Maintain a closer coordination and uphold a constant communication among the assigned officials who actively participate in planning, execution and monitoring of these projects in order to maximize its utilization and speed up its implementation.
observations.managementAction: Closer coordination and constant communication among the assigned officials were already undertaken in the implementation of LDF.

observations.recommendation: Require the CGSO to conduct a complete physical count  of PPE and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor. Advice the CAO to reconcile the PPE subsidiary records with that of the CGSO’s property cards based on the result of the count.
observations.managementAction: No Property ledger cards maintained by the City Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Partial Implement-ation | No report of the Physical Inventory was submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding CA.
observations.managementAction: Still with unliquidated cash advances
observations.reason: Laxity of personnel  with cash advances to submit liquidation

observations.recommendation: Require the CGSO to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City under his charge pursuant to Sections 375 & 490 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Land titles were already processed to most of the acquired donated lands.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO stop the accounting practice of treating   purchases of supplies as outright expenses.
observations.managementAction:
There were still supplies such as medicines, rice, etc purchased in bulk and were recorded/treated directly as expenses in the CY 2018

See Audit Finding No. 16 of this audit report
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Weak internal control system in managing supplies purchased in bulk.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has yet to coordinate with the LDRRMO for the possible compliance of the required report.
observations.reason: Management did not require the LDRRMO the submission of the aforecited report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the timely implementation of projects lined up for disaster preparedness and mainstream these in their activities to better prepare for and effectively mitigate the perilous impact of natural and man-made calamities.
observations.managementAction: The purchase of a rescue vehicle which was an outlined project for disaster preparedness will be undertaken in 2018.
observations.reason: The project had a continuing appropriation and is due for implementation in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials and employees plan and incur travel to only those which are urgent and extremely necessary and will involve the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some offices within the LGU adopted the cost-cutting measures to curb travelling expenses.
observations.reason: The measures which were undertaken may not be enough to make up for the continued costly practices that concern travels since in CY 2017, a supplemental budget was passed to cover the additional travelling expenses incurred.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management give justification for the above mentioned audit findings and follow the provision prescribed by Section 10, 48 and 53 Rule XVI of the IRR-A of Republic Act 9184. Further, competitive bidding must be used as a means of acquiring equipment and materials amounting to ₱50,000.00 and above in order to give equal chances among suppliers within the locality.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Chairman explained that it resorted to a negotiated procurement due to two failed biddings.
observations.reason: The BAC Chairman has yet to look into the documents (BAC Resolution on the two failed biddings) so that he can submit it to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management terminate the ₽15,441,158.56 deposit with the FCB and deposit the same to GFI with universal bank license and a CAMELS rating of  “3”.
observations.managementAction: After an inquiry by management with the FCB on the matter, it was able to present a document which showed that it was authorized by Central Bank to receive deposits from government agencies.
observations.reason: The First Consolidated Bank remains one of the depository banks of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that –
a. The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory taking of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU.
b. The Local Chief Executive should require the Inventory Committee to prepare the necessary inventory reports and submit a copy to the Auditor in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual.
c. The Municipal Accountant should effect adjustments of the discrepancies between the actual and recorded balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Initial steps included the creation of the Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Though it was agreed that each department/office will submit an Inventory Report on the PPE which are assigned/found in their respective jurisdiction, said report was not yet tendered as at year-end. The Inventory Committee was not able to conduct a physical inventory taking and consequently, no report was prepared for that matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management undertake a concerted effort to fast
track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of
the delay. We further recommended that the Municipality ensure that only projects
that are aligned with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017 are charged thereto in order that the desired socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved.

We recommended and management agreed that in the preparation of its
Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds to PPAs that are
responsive to the objective of providing protection to children against abuse,
neglect, exploitation and violence, and promotion of their rights and welfare as
envisioned by R.A. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the
Local Development Plan for Children and Children’s profile as required under
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed that the LSB refrain from
charging expenses not listed under Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and its related
DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular, review the PPAs included in the SEF
budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to
secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification that request for the use of SEF is
net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the
DepEd and the ECCD Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare the POW with
detailed engineering before prosecuting any project in accordance with the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to ensure that the same is properly carried out to avoid
wastage of government funds.

We recommended that management immediately transfer the bags of
cement to a secured place to prevent further damage and loss; account for all
deliveries and withdrawals of the bags of cement; maintain property cards and
submit a copy of the inventory report to the Auditor; and determine the number of
bags of cement stolen or damaged, identify the personnel liable therefor and require
them to pay for the money value thereof.

We recommended that management prioritize the establishment and
operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled
dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended
that all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and
segregation at source.

We recommended that management ensure the installation of signboards in
the project sites to provide the public information and guidance about the on-going
projects being undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and management agreed to properly allocate funds for the
implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We further recommended and which
management likewise agreed to immediately stop the use of GAD funds for
scholarship program and, henceforth, prioritize the implementation of programs
and activities which would promote gender equality.

We recommended that the use of the three units cargo truck for ordinary,
day-to-day operations be minimized in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles
for rescue and response activities in times of actual calamity in conformity with
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.

We recommend that management conduct inventory of insurable
properties and submit  listing  to  GSIS  for  evaluation/appraisal  of  the
insurance coverage and comply with RA 656 and Memorandum  Circular  No.  634
dated May 10, 1973 of the Office of the President.

We recommend that management:


   a) Exert effort through the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to reconstruct
      and update its property records and  reconcile  with  the  Accounting
      records to facilitate  the  setting  up  and  periodic  provision  of
      depreciation.

   b) Direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  adhere  to  the  policies  and
      guidelines of Section 4(o) of NGAS Vol. I and COA Circular No.  2003-
      007 to arrive at an accurate computation for a fair  presentation  of
      financial statements.

We recommend that, henceforth, management require from the  job  order
and contractual personnel the  submission  of  accomplishment  report  every
month and  to  direct  the  Human  Resource  Management  Officer  (HRMO)  to
indicate in the contract of service the specific job description or work  to
be performed by the job order and contractual personnel.

We recommend that management provide  a  venue  to  discuss  with  the
municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its  employees  are
able to receive a minimum of P4,000.00 take home pay per month in  pursuance
of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.


      Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO  monitor
payroll deductions and refuse to  sign  certifications  for  application  of
loans should an employee’s net pay fall below P4,000.00.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require   the   Municipal
Accountant and other concerned officials to submit,  as  soon  as  possible,
its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation the AAR 2017.

      It is further recommended that management comply  with  the  provision
and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt  of  the  AAR
CY 2017.
