We recommended that the designated focal person for every project/program funded by other government departments and offices must coordinate with the City Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office in order that receipts of trust funds can be accurately monitored.
We further recommended that the City Accounting Office must always be furnished with the Memorandum of Agreement and the corresponding Work and Financial Plan for each and every project/program in order to serve as the guidelines in the dispensing of funds and provide all the necessary information needed in the recording of the same particularly in the subsidiary ledgers.
We recommended that management to meticulously screen expenses charged against the Special Education Fund in order to maximize the utilization of the SEF for its purpose.

We recommended that all disbursement of funds under the SEF must be certified by the city superintendent of school as to its validity, propriety and legality before processing the same for the approval of the City Mayor (as the co-chairman of the LSB) and any payment can be made.
We recommended that recording of receipt of the share of the National Wealth must be on a cash basis as prescribed in the NGAS.

We recommended that management, particularly the BAC Secretariat, should submit the purchase orders and contracts; and each and every supporting document related to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects of the LGU, complete and not on installment basis, in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Sec. 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.
observations.managementAction: There is still a need to designate personnel to work on the processing of the documentary requirements pursuamt to DENR AO No.2016-21.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the rescue vehicles and heavy equipment from the Trust Fund to the appropriate account in the General Fund in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The rescue vehicle and heavy equipment acquired out of the LDRRMF recorded under Trust Fund books were already transferred to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials implement the programs immediately and undertake a concerted effort to address whatever obstacles that may hinder the implementation and completion of the project as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: Some DSWD Programs were implemented. The Construction of Day Care Centers under BUB Projects and the utilization of the AICs are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct from the CY 2017 electric subsidy due the beneficiaries if they still are unable to pay.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a recommendation to the Mayor to revert the outstanding payables which have been outstanding for more than two years, which the latter had indorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan. The request was approved per SB Res. No. 266, s. 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials undertake a concerted effort to implement PPAs outlined under the 20% EDF and hasten the completion of the project so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
observations.managementAction: There is lack of availability of personnel and equipment, bad weather condition, failure of bidding, and priority given to the implementation of BuB projects and other projects under the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts payables which have been outstanding for more than two years, and if found to have no valid claims against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan members act on the request of the Municipal Accountant by passing a resolution reverting the long outstanding payables without valid claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a recommendation to the Mayor to revert the accounts payable which have been outstanding for more than two years, and if found to have no valid claims against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity. The latter had indorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan. The same was approved per SB Res. No. 266, s. 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU appropriate one percent (1%) of its internal revenue allotment and utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and protect the welfare of children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: There is lack of personnel to implement the programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to immediately issue demand letters to the accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances. Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated amounts and to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Of the unliquidated cash advances, P80,655.00 were already demanded for liquidation. Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations on the grant of cash advances. P58,836.00 were already liquidated as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of PS-DBM Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Because it is more feasible and economical, the BAC issued a Resolution to purchase common-use supplies outside PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management secure necessary donation papers or deed of usufruct for the land where the multi-purpose center was constructed, if there is, otherwise initiate expropriation proceedings before the proper court to acquire the land upon payment of just compensation to protect the interest of the government. Henceforth, ensure the Municipality’s ownership over the land before undertaking any construction or introducing any improvement thereon to avoid unnecessary losses.
observations.managementAction: There is a need to designate personnel to negotiate and work on the documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: The Inventory Committee be required to complete the conduct of the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was required to complete the conduct of the physical count of properties. Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) were already maintained/updated.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that the Municipality of Valencia exerts efforts to trace the long outstanding Due from LGU account, and to write-off the long outstanding account, and to cause the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Mayor a recommendation to write-off the outstanding account which represents the claim from the Provincial Government of Negros Oriental as share in the 1992 franchise tax paid by NAPOCOR. Waiting for the Mayor’s request to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Treasurer of the Municipality keep tract of the unpaid revenues of the LGU and periodically send collection and/or demand letters to tax-payers with penalties & delinquent accounts; b. The Municipality avails of the civil remedies for tax delinquency collection provided under Section 174 of R.A. 7160 to prevent the accumulation of delinquent taxes receivable; c. Prepare the amendment of Section (3) c of Local Revenue Code of Valencia.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent. Submitted report on the collection of taxes to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office for the availment of civil remedies within the bounds of law.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that management: a. Charge the amount of P28,750.00 representing the cost of 23 bags at P1,250.00 to the head of the DSWD, Ms. Rowena C. Giangan, who was responsible for the purchase and non-distribution of the rice in the bodega; b. Undertake proper planning in rice distribution by purchasing only the realistic quantity of rice with target beneficiaries already identified; c. Stop the practice of giving dole-outs, rather encourage the constituents to be honorable and dignified in their way of living; d. Give projects that could provide employment to the locality.
observations.managementAction: Mrs. Rowena Giangan paid the amount representing the 23 bags upon her retirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite; b. Since the program has long been overdue, implement the Solid Waste Management program as soon as possible in order to protect the health of the constituents and the environment; c. Request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the inclusion in the Supplemental Budget or in the CY 2016 Annual Budget appropriations for Solid Waste Management Programs and its activities.
observations.managementAction: The Barangay Bagsakan Operator submitted the required status reports. Project proposal still for review and approval.
observations.reason: Problem with the availability of funds to operate the project and hiring of personnel to manage the project is not feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets; b. Require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be transferred to the appropriate account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS; c. Require the LGU to maintain a properly accomplished/update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: Subject properties were already transferred to the General Fund. PPELC were maintained.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Management terminate the MOA with the Valencia Community Development Cooperative; b. Management should operate the Barangay Bagsakan and train its staff to manage the same.
observations.managementAction: To provide the necessary technical assistance, letters were sent in CY 2016 to comply with the documentary requirements. Required the Operator to submit monthly reports on the status of the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan. Also, demand letters were sent in CY 2016 to the municipal officials and personnel to pick up the items at the Municipal office. The Barangay Bagsakan Operator submitted the income statement and financial balance sheet for December 2016.
observations.reason: Insufficient funds to operate the project and the hiring of personnel to manage the project is not feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Prepare and submit immediately to the auditor the income statement and financial balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, by requiring the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary data/information and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month. b. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: The Barangay Bagsakan Operator submitted the income statement and financial balance sheet on the operation as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, no financial statements were submitted to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist regularly report to and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant any addition to or reduction from the Work/Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Accounts. We likewise recommend for the conduct of a physical count of the livestock to determine their existence which shall be reconciled with the Livestock Ledger Card pursuant to Section 442 of the GAAM, Volume II. We further Instructed the Municipal Agriculturist to hasten the processing of requests for relief of accountability for dead animals and when granted, the same may be dropped from the books of accounts in order to present fairly the asset account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist reported to the Municipal Livestock Inspector to hasten the processing of documents for the request for relief of accountability for dead animals.
observations.reason: There is need to reorganize the composition of the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality maintains a single stockroom with adequate storage space for all materials and a responsible storekeeper be assigned thereat. The stockroom should be secured against pilferage, unauthorized use and protected from deteriorating effects of the elements; b. The materials in the stockroom should be properly arranged in such a way as to facilitate retrieval when needed and ensure that fresh usable items are always available. Materials should be issued on first in, first out basis; c. The storekeeper should keep bin cards for each type of article and hang on shelves or bins where the articles are stored. Reconcile bin cards with the stock/property cards maintained by the stock clerk and with the physical count of stocks on hand; d. The Office of the municipal treasurer should maintain stock cards for each class of article. All receipts, issuances and balances on hand of supplies and materials should be kept up-to-date. The stock cards serve as guide in preparing estimates of the quantity and description of articles needed to avoid overstocking. e. Conduct an immediate inventory-taking of all the items, prepare and submit inventory report to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: Required the Municipal Motorpool/Warehouse to maintain bin and stock cards. The stockroom was organized. Conducted inventory taking and submitted report to the auditor.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Project Coordinator evaluate and monitor the program, form a team to conduct inspection and inquiry on the electrical installation status of borrowers, and investigate reasons for the non-installation. We further recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection efforts by instructing the electric subsidy in charge to require clearance from the EILP coordinator of any loan balance of the subsidy claimant, demand immediate payment for overdue account, and/or institute legal remedies, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Collection was stopped because the electric subsidy was discontinued.
observations.reason: Collection was stopped because the electric subsidy was discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We required the accountant to send written reminders to employees with unliquidated cash advances and to thereafter impose the sanctions allowed under COA Circular No. 96-004. Henceforth, control the grant of cash advances by complying strictly with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, and P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant sent written reminders to employees with unliquidated cash advances and complied with the rules and regulations on the grant of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Facilitate the titling of the lots owned by the Municipality, so that the Municipality’s ownership thereto could be properly secured and to avoid future cases filed in court against the Municipality by unknown claimants. b. Apply for special patent of its real property requirements per DENR AO No. II pursuant to RA 10023.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Valencia had been applying for special documentary requirements per DENR AO No. II pursuant to RA 10023. Required the Insufficient documentary requirements per DENR AO No.II.
observations.reason: There is need to designate a focal person to work on the documentary requirements per DENR AO No. 2016-21.

observations.managementAction: Except for emergency procurement of food supplies at the Badiang Spring Resort and Hotel which were charged to its revolving fund, all procurements of LGU Valencia were coursed through the Bids and Awards Committee.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend management to see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be promptly and fully implemented as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated thereby improving the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Programs under the 20% Development Fund which remained unimplemented as of the end of 2016 were in the priority of implementatio n for the ensuing year.
observations.reason: The municipality was not able to implement some projects (out of the 2017 Development Fund) in the last quarter of CY 2017 as it hosted the Provincial Meet. The Office of the Municipal Engineer was engaged in the preparation of facilities to be used in the said event.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
1. Adhere to the provisions of Section 3G02 and 3G03 of the Revenue Code of Valencia so as to discourage delayed payment of water bills, minimize, if not eliminate delinquencies and realize the income due to the government for a particular period.  The Valencia Waterworks System in-charge shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in this regard.
2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect from the delinquent water consumers the amount due to the LGU pertaining to the uncollected income due to unimposed penalties.
3. Issue Notices of Disconnection for delinquent accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer was required to collect the penalty and surcharges due from delinquent consumers.  The water services of consumers whose account had become delinquent for a long time were disconnected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a. Direct all collectors of the municipality to remit their collections intact daily to the Municipal Treasurer, pursuant to Section 15 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the accumulation of unremitted collections by collectors.
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that collectors are not issued additional accountable forms unless the collections pertaining to the accountable forms previously issued to them are remitted intact or an inventory of any accountable form had been diligently made.
observations.managementAction: Management ordered the erring collectors (who were involved in the delayed remittance of collections) to stop from performing their collecting function.  However, on some period (business tax season) when several taxpayers would pay their dues, these collectors were asked to resume their function.
observations.reason: The daily turnover of collections by the collectors was not strictly imposed and monitored by the municipal treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were both informed on the matter and were further instructed to find means to comply with the recommendatio n.
observations.reason: Although the receipt and issuance of office supplies was taken charged by one department, the LGU has yet to establish an effective internal control in so far as management of its food supplies at the Badiang Spring Hotel and Resort is concerned.

observations.recommendation: Require the management to ensure prompt and full implementation of the development projects as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose which the fund was appropriated and improve the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of National Government Agency-funded projects were given immediate priority.
observations.reason: Projects lined up under the 20% Development Fund were deferred to give way on the immediate implementation of programs whose funding sources were the National Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require from the collecting officers that revenue activities, if applicable, be processed through the Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System (ETRACS).
observations.managementAction: The office concerned was instructed to make use of the existing system in their collection activities.
observations.reason: Real Property Tax Collections are manually executed since data pertaining to the amounts collectible are yet to be entered into the system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement projects lined up for disaster preparedness and mainstream these in their activities to better prepare for and lessen the impact  in case of natural and man-made hazards/disasters.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is focusing its disaster risk reduction activities in the training of emergency response team.
observations.reason: DILG (BUB)-funded projects included the flood control construction which was one of the major projects for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the preparer of the request to list the food items with complete and specific descriptions.
observations.managementAction: For activities and functions catered by the LGU-owned Badiang Hotel, a complete detail of the food items served to the participants had already been provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned LGU officials coordinate with and request from the office of Congressman Arthur Yap the necessary documents to effect the recording in the books the cost of the donated firetruck and to register the same as LGU-Valencia’s property. Otherwise, appraisal may be made as basis in recording thereof.
observations.managementAction: Several follow ups were made to the office of Congressman Yap to secure the pertinent documents for the recording in the books the subject fire truck. As at year end however, no document was furnished yet.
observations.reason: The Office of the Accountant was only able to record the cost of the water tank that was installed to the fire truck. It was resolved that an appraisal on the equipment will be undertaken to come up with a basis in the recording thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive draw a list of three (3) recommendees and submit the same to the Secretary of Finance who will appoint a permanent Municipal Treasurer who will supervise the treasury office and exercise proper management of funds and property of the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has already requested from an applicant (who was recommended to the position of Municipal Treasurer) the pertinent documents for submission to the Secretary of the Department of Finance.
observations.reason: The appointment of the new Municipal Treasurer is on process and she is due to assume within the CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
• Immediately stop the current practice in recording transactions involving petty cash fund and strictly follow the prescribed imprest system;
• Ensure that sufficient internal control is in place by requiring quarterly reconciliation of the Petty cash book and petty cash on hand, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: In actual practice, the replenishment of the petty cash fund was treated as liquidation of cash advances. Also, there were expenses charged to the alleged PCF which were not petty.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management should strictly follow the foregoing provisions in complying the necessary documentary requirements in processing payments or disbursements of financial assistance to NGOs.
observations.managementAction:
There were still transfer of funds to NGOs with inadequate documentations in CY 2017.
(Jaycees and Bukidnon Chamber of Commerce)
observations.reason: Still lacking supporting documents

observations.recommendation:
a.	Strictly observe the actual delivery dates in comparison to the delivery period and deduct liquidating damages immediately if deem necessary especially when it is apparent that the supplier incurs delays upon delivery in conformity to the specific provisions cited in the General Conditions of a Contract;

b.	Always be conscious of the marked date in every documentary requirement involved in every procurement process; Ensure that documents are dated in proper chronological order and in accordance to what Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as Government Procurement Reform Act demands.
observations.managementAction: The Management emphasized that liquidated damages were already computed in cases of supplier’s delays and  strict monitoring on the chronological order of dates was already practiced, however, still there were little lapses that cannot be avoided.

Filed request for relief for the damaged rice worth P50,000.00.

Relief was granted

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the total implementation of the prioritized projects funded from the 20% Local Development Fund.  Maintain a closer coordination and uphold a constant communication among the assigned officials who actively participate in planning, execution and monitoring of these projects in order to maximize its utilization and speed up its implementation as per CAIP to ensure that intended beneficiaries could enjoy optimum benefits at the earliest time possible.
observations.managementAction: Closer coordination and constant communication among the assigned officials were already undertaken in the implementation of LDF.

observations.recommendation: Require the CGSO to conduct a complete physical count  of PPE and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor. Advice the CAO to reconcile the PPE subsidiary records with that of the CGSO’s property cards based on the result of the count.
observations.managementAction: The GSO had already conducted the Physical Inventory for CY 2016 in coordination with Budget Office and Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Partially Im-plemented

observations.recommendation: The Accountant exert efforts to secure copies of the bank validated deposits slips and bank debit and credit memoranda pertinent to the alleged reconciling items from the depository banks.  As regards the alleged deposit in transit, the liquidating officer who made the alleged deposits  submit copies of the bank validated deposit slips for verification and/or confirmation with the banks. Enjoin the City Accountant to investigate the exceptions noted in the periodic bank reconciliation statements and to effect immediately proper adjustments to the accounts for items that proved valid.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accounting Department prepared and submitted updated Bank Reconciliation Statements, however, still with numerous reconciling items which remained as such as of the end of December 2017,

Still an audit finding in this audit report.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding CA.  Endeavor to keep the granting and utilization of CAs strictly within the limits of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Institute appropriate sanction. Their failure to liquidate shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding or suspension of their salaries and other emoluments.

Regarding the CA of  deceased official,   request for write-off  pursuant to the 2009 revised Rules of Procedure of the COA and COA Resolution No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Still with unliquidated cash advances
observations.reason: Due to uncontrolled cash advances granted to officers and employees as discussed in Audit Finding No.   __ in this audit report.

observations.recommendation: Follow-up with the concerned local government officials on the titling of the properties in favor of the LGU Valencia City to avoid possible disputes in the future.  Instruct CAO personnel handling the account to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported. Require the CGSO to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City under his charge pursuant to Sections 375 & 490 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Land titles were already processed to most of the acquired donated lands.

observations.recommendation: The LCE make representation with the SP to promulgate a city ordinance for the optimum utilization of the City Government’s share of the proceeds from the development and utilization of the national wealth. Consider funding power generating projects like solar power that could respond immediately to the needs of City constituents for lower cost electricity

We recommended and that the CAO stop the accounting practice of treating   purchases of supplies as outright expenses. We further recommended to  adopt the inventory system of supplies and materials, and utilize the newly constructed bodega  to store the delivered items for safekeeping and issuance to various end-users.
observations.managementAction: The LGU partially implemented the inventory system for supplies and some other material for starting CY 2016.
observations.reason: Inventory system was selectively effected to several inventory accounts.

observations.managementAction: The recommendation was already forwarded to the monitoring committee for validation and verification.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed  that: 

The Local Finance Committee recommend to the Sangguniang  Panglungsod appropriate tax revisions on rates of real property taxes, business fees and charges, including its penalties and surcharges in accordance with Section 191 of RA7160;

Enact the City Market Code to enhance collections, include other economic activities that was not anticipated before and  to improve the management of  economic enterprises, conformably to Section 17 of RA7160; and
Expedite tax mapping, utilizing geographical information system on real properties and business establishments
.
observations.reason: Implementation will be on the ensuing year although it has been duly approved during the current year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accounting Office should strictly follow the COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and faithfully comply with the necessary documents needed in payroll preparations and to stop the practice of accommodating certain officials in the inclusion in the PACS Module payroll even without DTRs and drop the names of employees who were not able to submit Daily Time Record within the prescribed cut-off period set by the HRMO.

We have recommended that the Head of Procuring Entity (the City Mayor) to send a letter to the contractors requiring them to 
comply with their obligation to post warranty security over the fully accepted projects made by them. 

Also, instruct the BAC to include in the Invitation to BID posting of Warranty Security as additional documents to be complied with.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Accountant of the Municipality of Valencia exerts efforts to trace the long outstanding Due from LGU account, and cause the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU: a. Prepare and submit immediately to the auditor the income statement and the balance sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of December 31, 2012, by requiring the operator to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the necessary data/information and records. Thereafter, the financial reports shall be prepared and submitted every 5th of each month. b. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports as well as the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

            We recommend that Management:

   1. Refrain from resorting to procurement  and  disbursement  through  the
      reimbursement  scheme.   Acquisition  of   goods   and   services   is
      recommended to   pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant
      to R.A. 9184 and disbursement procedures in accordance with the NGAS;


   2.  Pay directly to supplier, legitimate payee or concerned persons;


   3.  Comply with the  requirement  of  adequate  procurement  planning  in
      accordance with Section 7 of R.A.9184.

Recommendation:

      In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management see to it  that
the 20%  Development  Fund  shall  be  promptly  and  fully  implemented  as
programmed in order to achieve the  intended  purpose  which  the  fund  was
appropriated  thereby  improving  the   socio-economic   and   environmental
condition of the constituents.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the municipal officials and employees plan and incur
travels to only those which are urgent  and  extremely  necessary  and  will
involve the minimum expenditure and  are  beneficial  to  the  municipality.
Furthermore, the grant  of  authority  to  travel  as  well  as  claims  for
travelling expenses have to be regulated as to the relevance  and  necessity
of the purpose of the travel.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to  submit  the  Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment  Plan  (LDRRMFIP)  at
the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and  Utilization  of
DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on  or  before  the
15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management consider  the  timely  implementation  of
projects lined up for disaster preparedness and mainstream  these  in  their
activities to better prepare  for  and  effectively  mitigate  the  perilous
impact of natural and man-made calamities.

Recommendations:

      It is recommended that management:


1. Adhere to the provisions of Section 3G02 and 3G03 of the Revenue Code  of
   Valencia so as to discourage delayed payment of water bills, minimize, if
   not eliminate delinquencies and realize the income due to the  government
   for a particular period.  The Valencia Waterworks System  incharge  shall
   coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in this regard.


2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer  to  collect  from  the  delinquent  water
   consumers the amount due to the LGU pertaining to the uncollected  income
   due to unimposed penalties.


3. Issue Notices of Disconnection for delinquent accounts, if warranted.

Recommendations:

      We recommend that management:


     a. Direct all collectors of the municipality to remit their collections
       intact daily to the Municipal Treasurer, pursuant to  Section  15  of
       the New Government Accounting System Manual, in order to preclude the
       accumulation of unremitted collections by collectors.


     b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it  that  collectors  are
       not  issued  additional  accountable  forms  unless  the  collections
       pertaining to the accountable forms previously  issued  to  them  are
       remitted intact or an inventory of  any  accountable  form  had  been
       diligently made.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the management  give  justification  for  the  above
mentioned audit findings and follow  the  provision  prescribed  by  Section
Section 10, 48  and  53  Rule  XVI  of  the  IRR-A  of  Republic  Act  9184.
Further, competitive bidding must be used as a means of acquiring  equipment
and materials amounting to P50,000.00 and  above  in  order  to  give  equal
chances among suppliers within the locality.

We have recommended that the Management should strictly follow the foregoing provisions in complying the necessary documentary requirements in processing payments or disbursements of financial assistance to NGOs.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the total implementation of the prioritized projects funded from the 20% Local Development Fund.  Maintain a closer coordination and uphold a constant communication among the assigned officials who actively participate in planning, execution and monitoring of these projects in order to maximize its utilization and speed up its implementation as per CAIP to ensure that intended beneficiaries could enjoy optimum benefits at the earliest time possible.

We recommended that management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of Certificates of Title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain their respective property records for each real property.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the rescue vehicles and heavy equipment from the Trust Fund books to the appropriate account in the General Fund in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LGU officials implement the programs immediately and undertake a concerted effort to address whatever obstacles that may hinder the implementation and completion of the project as scheduled.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately send demand letters to the beneficiaries with outstanding loans receivable of more than five years pursuant to Section 3 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2001-3. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct from the electric subsidy due the beneficiaries if they still are unable to pay.

We recommended that municipal officials undertake a concerted effort to implement PPAs outlined under the 20% EDF and hasten the completion of the project so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts payables which have been outstanding for more than two years, and if found to have no valid claims against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity. We also recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan members act on the request of the Municipal Accountant by passing a resolution reverting the long outstanding payables without valid claims.

We recommended that management appropriate one percent (1%) of its internal revenue allotment and utilize the same for programs, projects and activities that would safeguard and protect the welfare of children in accordance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately issue demand letters to the accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances. Henceforth, strictly observe the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of advances to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated amounts and to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.

observations.reason: The Municipality has started the construction of additional storage facility. The stockroom measures and storekeeper assignment are being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Project Coordinator evaluate and monitor the program, form a team to conduct inspection and inquiry on the electrical installation status of borrowers, and investigate reasons for the non-installation. We further recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection efforts by instructing the electric subsidy in charge to require clearance from the EILP coordinator of any loan balance of the subsidy claimant, demand immediate payment for overdue account, and/or institute legal remedies, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The MTO has already started deducting collectable amount from payment from borrowers. The municipal treasurer has already started deducting from electric subsidy refunds for EILP beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Significant amount is still collectable from borrowers.

observations.recommendation: Require the Committee on Inventory to prepare and submit the Inventory and WMR and the 135 Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59 of the New Government Accounting System Manual, Volume II in order that disposal and dropping of the properties from the books of accounts can be initiated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Require the accountant to send written reminders to employees with unliquidated cash advances and to write-off the dormant account and thereafter impose the sanctions allowed under COA Circular No. 96-004. Henceforth, require the accountant to control the grant of cash advances by complying strictly with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, and P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will request for an authority from COA for the write-off of the dormant account. Henceforth, require the accountant to send written reminders to employees with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Legal Officer, Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the titling of the lots owned by the municipality, so that the municipality’s patents of ownership thereto could be properly secured and to avoid future cases filed in court against the municipality by avail of the unknown claimants. Similar observation is reiterated in AOM No. 2017-05 dated May 5, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has coordinated with DENR-CENRO II, the office assign to handle special patents of real properties pursuant to R.A. No. 10023. The Municipality will avail of the streamlined requirements for LGU and is now awaiting the application form for the purpose.
